Sweet version: later you notice that after the sprint, the cat jumps onto your bed and curls into a ball by your feet. Turns out the finish line of that midnight race was always going to be you.

items.sourceNote: 貓咪吐毛球前的乾嘔聲與選地點吐的行為，養貓人共同經驗，原創主子語錄

zh-tw.content:
半夜，黑暗中，
傳來那個聲音。

咯⋯⋯咯⋯⋯咯⋯⋯

你瞬間清醒。
你知道那是什麼。
那是主子，要吐毛球了。

你光速跳下床，
抱起牠想衝到磁磚地。

太遲了。

主子早就算好了——
牠選的地點，
永遠是地毯。
永遠是棉被。
永遠不會是，
那塊好擦的磁磚。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個一聽到「咯咯咯」就會從睡夢中彈起來、然後永遠慢一步的養貓人——主子的選址，從來不是隨機的
variations: 進階版：你後來買了一塊地墊放在牠常吐的地方。從那天起，主子改在旁邊三公分的地方吐。那塊地墊，至今乾乾淨淨。
冷知識版：科學家說，毛球是貓咪理毛時吞下的毛累積而成。但沒有任何一篇論文，能解釋為什麼牠們總是挑最貴的那塊地毯。

en.content:
Middle of the night. In the dark,
that sound.

Hrk... hrk... hrk...

You are instantly awake.
You know exactly what it is.
The cat is about to bring up a hairball.

You leap out of bed at light speed,
scoop it up, racing for the tile floor.

Too late.

The cat planned this all along —
the spot it picks
is always the rug.
Always the comforter.
Never, ever
the easy-to-wipe tile.
en.editorNote: Send to the owner who bolts upright the instant they hear 'hrk hrk hrk' and is forever one step too slow — the cat's choice of location was never random
variations: Advanced version: you put a wipeable mat down on its usual spot. From that day on, the cat throws up exactly three inches to the side. That mat remains spotless to this day.
Fun fact version: scientists say hairballs form from fur swallowed during grooming. But no paper has ever explained why they always target the most expensive rug in the house.

items.sourceNote: 貓咪一看到外出籠就消失、要帶去看醫生的行為，養貓人共同經驗，原創主子語錄

zh-tw.content:
你只是，把外出籠，從櫃子裡，拿出來。

你還沒打開。
你什麼都還沒說。

但主子，已經不見了。

那隻平常黏在你腳邊、
你走到哪牠跟到哪的貓，
此刻，徹底地，從這個宇宙，消失了。

你找遍全家。
床底下，沒有。
衣櫃裡，沒有。

最後，你在一個
你自己都不知道存在的縫隙裡，
找到了牠。

牠看著你的眼神寫著：
我們之間的信任，到此為止。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個每次帶貓看醫生、都要先上演一場全屋搜索的養貓人——主子對外出籠的雷達，比任何 GPS 都準
variations: 進階版：你學乖了，把外出籠藏在陽台，提前三天放好，假裝它只是一個普通的家具。第四天你要用的時候，主子還是消失了。牠不是看到籠子才跑——牠是看到「你今天眼神不太一樣」。
甜版：看完醫生回家，主子氣了你整整一個下午，躲在角落不理人。但到了晚上睡覺時間，牠還是默默跳上床，貼著你睡。原來再大的氣，也大不過想靠近你的本能。

en.content:
You simply take the carrier
out of the closet.

You haven't opened it.
You haven't said a word.

But the cat is already gone.

The cat that normally clings to your ankles,
that follows you everywhere you go,
has, at this moment, completely vanished
from this universe.

You search the whole house.
Under the bed — nothing.
In the wardrobe — nothing.

Finally you find it
in a gap you didn't even know
your home contained.

The look in its eyes says:
the trust between us ends here.
en.editorNote: Send to the owner who has to run a full-house manhunt every time the vet visit comes around — the cat's radar for the carrier beats any GPS
variations: Advanced version: you wise up and hide the carrier on the balcony three days early, pretending it's just ordinary furniture. On day four when you need it, the cat vanishes anyway. It wasn't reacting to the carrier — it was reacting to 'your eyes look a little different today.'

Sweet version: home from the vet, the cat sulks at you for an entire afternoon, hiding in a corner. But come bedtime, it quietly hops onto the bed and presses up against you to sleep. Turns out no grudge is bigger than the instinct to be near you.

items.sourceNote: 貓咪舔著舔著突然咬一口的 love bite 行為，養貓人共同經驗，原創主子語錄

zh-tw.content:
主子，主動，靠過來。
躺在你的手邊。
開始，舔你的手。

你心想：天啊，今天牠好乖。
你整個人，融化了。

就在你最放鬆的那一秒——

牠咬了你一口。

不重。
但很明確。

然後，牠繼續舔。
彷彿，剛剛那一口，
是這套溫柔流程裡，
完全合理的，一個步驟。

你終於懂了：
主子的愛，從來就不是純的。
是甜的，裡面，包著一顆，牙。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個被主子舔到一半突然咬、然後一臉問號的養貓人——這不是攻擊，這是牠表達愛的一種獨特方式（雖然很痛）
variations: 進階版：你查了資料，說這叫「愛的咬咬（love bite）」，是貓咪過度興奮或太享受的表現。但你還是不懂，為什麼表達「我很愛你」，需要動用到牙齒。
甜版：後來你發現，牠只對你這樣。對其他人，牠連碰都不給碰。原來那一口，是主子給你的，一張，限量的，會員卡。

en.content:
The cat comes over on its own.
Lies down by your hand.
Starts to lick it.

You think: oh my, it's being so sweet today.
You melt completely.

And in the very second you're most relaxed —

it bites you.

Not hard.
But very clearly.

Then it goes right back to licking.
As if that bite
were a perfectly reasonable step
in this tender little routine.

You finally get it:
the cat's love was never pure.
It's sweet, with a tooth
tucked inside.
en.editorNote: Send to the owner who gets licked and then suddenly bitten mid-cuddle and is left utterly confused — this isn't an attack, it's the cat's peculiar love language (it just happens to hurt)
variations: Advanced version: you look it up. It's called a 'love bite,' a sign of overstimulation or pure enjoyment. You still don't understand why expressing 'I love you' requires the use of teeth.
Sweet version: later you realize it only does this to you. With everyone else, it won't even let them touch it. Turns out that little bite was a limited-edition membership card, issued to you alone.

items.sourceNote: 貓咪凌晨三點暴衝（FRAP/zoomies）行為，養貓人共同惡夢，原創主子語錄

zh-tw.content:
凌晨三點。
整間屋子，安靜得像深海。

突然——

一陣急促的腳步聲，
從走廊那頭，
以時速一百，衝了過來。

碰一聲，撞到牆。
再碰一聲，撞到櫃子。
床頭那杯水，倒了。

你睜開眼，什麼都沒看到。
只有黑暗中，
一雙發光的眼睛，
以及一條，正在劇烈擺動的尾巴。

三秒後，
牠消失了。
彷彿剛剛的一切，
從未發生。

而你，再也睡不著了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個每天被主子凌晨暴衝吵醒、然後一臉死灰的養貓人——這叫 FRAP，是貓咪釋放積壓一整天能量的儀式，無解，只能認命
variations: 進階版：你查資料，說這叫「貓咪的狩獵本能」，凌晨是牠們野外祖先出沒的時間。你看著眼前這隻連蒼蠅都打不到的主子，決定不跟牠提這件事。
甜版：後來你習慣了。凌晨三點那陣腳步聲，從吵醒你，變成一種安心。因為你知道，那是牠還在，活蹦亂跳地，在你身邊。

en.content:
3 a.m.
The whole house is as quiet as the deep sea.

Then — suddenly —

a burst of frantic footsteps
comes tearing down the hallway
at a hundred miles an hour.

Thud — into the wall.
Thud again — into the cabinet.
The glass of water on the nightstand goes over.

You open your eyes. You see nothing.
Just, in the dark,
a pair of glowing eyes
and a tail whipping violently back and forth.

Three seconds later,
it's gone.
As if none of it
ever happened.

And you? You're never falling back asleep.
en.editorNote: Send to the owner who gets jolted awake by the 3 a.m. zoomies every single night — it's called a FRAP, the cat's nightly ritual for unloading a day's worth of stored energy. There is no cure. Only acceptance
variations: Advanced version: you look it up. It's the cat's 'hunting instinct,' and 3 a.m. is when its wild ancestors roamed. You look at this cat that can't even land a hit on a housefly and decide not to bring it up.

Sweet version: eventually you get used to it. Those 3 a.m. footsteps go from waking you up to oddly reassuring. Because you know it means the cat is still here, bouncing off the walls, right beside you.

items.sourceNote: 貓咪坐在筆電鍵盤上、擋住螢幕的行為，遠端工作者共同經驗，原創主子語錄

zh-tw.content:
你打開筆電，準備工作。

三秒鐘內，
主子精準地，
降落在鍵盤正中央。

螢幕被牠的屁股，完全擋住。
游標，因為牠的尾巴，亂跳。
你的文件，多了一行「mmmmmmmmmmmm」。

你以為牠是想取暖。
你以為牠是想陪你。

不。

牠是在宣示主權。
用體溫，蓋過你的；
用氣味，覆蓋你的。

牠在說：
「這台電腦是我的。
這張桌子是我的。
還有，你，也是我的。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個在家工作、筆電永遠有一隻貓的人——這不是撒嬌，根據研究，這是主子在標記領地，意思是「我擁有你」
variations: 進階版：你買了一個一模一樣溫熱的寵物保暖墊，放在旁邊，想引牠過去。牠看了一眼，然後更堅定地，坐回你的鍵盤上。牠要的從來不是溫度，是你的注意力。
甜版：視訊會議開到一半，主子的臉突然出現在鏡頭裡。同事們笑成一團。你假裝困擾，其實心裡偷偷想：這大概是這場會議，唯一的亮點。

en.content:
You open your laptop, ready to work.

Within three seconds,
the cat lands
dead center on the keyboard.

The screen is fully blocked by its rear end.
The cursor jumps around thanks to its tail.
Your document gains a fresh line of 'mmmmmmmmmmmm.'

You think it wants warmth.
You think it wants company.

No.

It is asserting sovereignty.
Covering your heat with its own,
overwriting your scent with its own.

It is saying:
'This computer is mine.
This desk is mine.
And you — you are mine too.'
en.editorNote: Send to the work-from-home friend whose laptop always has a cat on it — this isn't affection. Research says it's the cat marking territory, and the message is 'I own you'
variations: Advanced version: you buy an identical heated pet pad and set it right beside you, hoping to lure the cat over. It glances at the pad, then settles back onto your keyboard with extra conviction. It was never about the warmth. It's about your attention.

Sweet version: mid video call, the cat's face suddenly fills the frame. Your coworkers crack up. You pretend to be annoyed, but secretly you think: that's probably the only highlight of this whole meeting.

items.sourceNote: 貓咪無視昂貴貓窩、執著於紙箱的行為，養貓人共同經驗，原創主子語錄

zh-tw.content:
你花了一千多塊，
買了一個評價超高的貓窩。
柔軟、保暖、有設計感。

主子走過去，
聞了一下，
轉身離開。

那個貓窩，
後來變成了你放雜物的地方。

然後，
你收網購包裹，
隨手把空紙箱丟在地上。

主子，
眼睛亮了。

牠跳進那個，
又破、又小、邊緣還黏著膠帶的紙箱，
滿足地，瞇起了眼。

你終於明白：
主子的快樂，
從來不能用金錢，衡量。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個買遍各種高級貓窩、結果主子只愛紙箱的養貓人——根據觀察，紙箱的密閉感給貓安全感，而你的錢包，只給你心痛感
variations: 進階版：你不死心，又買了一個更貴的。主子依然不理。你嘆了口氣，把它的外包裝紙箱留了下來。主子立刻住了進去。原來你真正該買的，是那個箱子。
甜版：你蹲下來，看著瞇眼縮在小破箱裡的主子，突然覺得，幸福原來這麼簡單。一個剛剛好的空間，加上一個牠信任的人，就夠了。

en.content:
You spent thirty bucks
on a top-rated cat bed.
Soft, warm, beautifully designed.

The cat walks over,
gives it one sniff,
turns around, and leaves.

That cat bed
later becomes the place you dump random clutter.

Then
a package arrives,
and you toss the empty cardboard box on the floor.

The cat's
eyes light up.

It hops into that
beat-up, too-small box with tape still stuck to the edge
and contentedly
squints its eyes shut.

You finally understand:
a cat's happiness
can never be measured in money.
en.editorNote: Send to the owner who's bought every fancy cat bed only to watch the cat live in a cardboard box — the enclosed space makes cats feel safe, and your wallet feel pain
variations: Advanced version: you refuse to give up and buy an even pricier bed. The cat ignores it again. You sigh and keep the box it shipped in. The cat moves in immediately. Turns out the box was the thing you should've ordered all along.

Sweet version: you crouch down and watch the cat squinting happily in its dingy little box, and it hits you how simple contentment really is. A space that fits just right, plus a person it trusts. That's enough.

items.sourceNote: 貓咪滿碗食物卻喵喵叫說沒得吃的行為，養貓人共同經驗，原創主子語錄

zh-tw.content:
主子站在飼料碗前，
抬起頭，
用一種，瀕臨餓死的眼神，
看著你喵喵叫。

你走過去看。

碗裡，
還有食物。

不是沒有。
是還有半碗。

你問牠：「這不就是飼料嗎？」

主子的表情寫著：
「那是『舊的』。
碗底那一層，我不吃。
我要『新的』。
從同一個袋子，倒出來的，新的。」

你倒了。
一模一樣的飼料。

主子，
滿意地，吃了起來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個飼料碗明明有東西、主子還是叫著說餓的養貓人——這叫「碗底焦慮」，貓咪就是要吃新鮮倒出來的那一份，哪怕完全一樣
variations: 進階版：你研究發現，這可能跟貓咪不喜歡食物碰到碗緣、或單純喜歡新鮮感有關。但你心裡清楚，真正的原因是——牠就是想使喚你一下。
甜版：你一邊碎念「明明就有」，一邊還是默默幫牠倒了新的。因為你發現，能讓牠這樣理直氣壯地對你撒嬌討食的，全世界，也只有你一個。

en.content:
The cat stands at its food bowl,
lifts its head,
and meows at you
with the eyes of a creature on the brink of starvation.

You walk over to check.

The bowl
still has food in it.

Not empty.
Half full, in fact.

You ask it: 'Isn't that literally your food?'

The cat's expression reads:
'That is the OLD food.
The layer at the bottom — I don't eat that.
I want NEW food.
Poured fresh, from the very same bag.'

You pour it.
The exact same kibble.

The cat,
satisfied,
begins to eat.
en.editorNote: Send to the owner whose cat cries that it's starving while the bowl is visibly not empty — it's 'bowl-bottom anxiety,' and the cat demands a freshly poured serving even if it's identical
variations: Advanced version: you read up on it. Maybe it's about food touching the rim of the bowl, or just a preference for freshness. But deep down you know the real reason — it simply wanted to make you do something.

Sweet version: grumbling 'there's clearly food in there,' you quietly pour the fresh batch anyway. Because you realize you're the only one in the whole world it gets to boss around for snacks this shamelessly.

items.sourceNote: 貓咪要求開門、出去後又立刻想進來的「門口糾結」行為，養貓人共同經驗，原創主子語錄

zh-tw.content:
主子站在門口，
喵喵叫，
要你開門。

你開了。

牠走到門檻，
停下。

一隻腳，跨在門外。
一隻腳，留在門內。

牠看看外面，
又回頭看看裡面，
陷入了一場，
人類無法理解的，重大抉擇。

三十秒過去了。
你還扶著門。

最後，
牠收回那隻腳，
轉身，走回屋裡。

彷彿剛剛叫你開門的，
是另外一隻貓。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個被主子要求開門、結果牠在門口站三十秒最後又不出去的養貓人——這個世紀難題，叫做「薛丁格的貓，到底要不要出門」
variations: 進階版：你以為這是個案。後來你發現，每一隻貓，都會。牠們不是真的想出去，牠們只是想確認——這扇門，是不是隨時，都會為牠而開。
甜版：你雖然覺得好氣又好笑，但每次牠在門口喵叫，你還是會立刻放下手邊的事去開門。因為你知道，牠要的不是那扇門，是你願意為牠開門的，那份心意。

en.content:
The cat stands at the door,
meowing,
demanding you open it.

You do.

It walks to the threshold
and stops.

One paw outside.
One paw inside.

It looks out,
then looks back in,
caught in a momentous decision
no human could ever comprehend.

Thirty seconds pass.
You're still holding the door.

Finally
it pulls that paw back,
turns around, and walks back inside.

As if the cat that just demanded the door
were a completely different cat.
en.editorNote: Send to the owner who opens the door on command only to watch the cat stand there for thirty seconds and not go out — this is the great riddle of our age: Schrödinger's cat, to leave or not to leave
variations: Advanced version: you assume it's just your cat. Then you learn every cat does it. They don't actually want to go out — they just need to confirm that the door will open for them, anytime they wish.

Sweet version: it's equal parts maddening and hilarious, but every time it meows at the door you still drop what you're doing to open it. Because you know it isn't the door it wants — it's knowing you're willing to open it.

items.sourceNote: 貓咪無視昂貴貓窩、只睡紙箱的經典行為，養貓人共同經驗，原創主子語錄

zh-tw.content:
你花了一千二，
買了一個五星級貓窩。
柔軟、保暖、附頂篷，
評論四點八顆星。

主子聞了一下，
走開。

然後牠鑽進，
那個貓窩寄來時，
裝它的紙箱。

並且，睡得很香。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個剛買貴貓窩、結果主子只愛紙箱的養貓人——這是養貓界最古老的詛咒：你買的牠不睡，包裝牠不放
variations: 進階版：後來你學乖了。你不再買貓窩，你直接留紙箱。主子很滿意。你的錢包也是。
甜版：你嘴上抱怨牠浪費你的錢，但你還是把那個紙箱留了下來，因為牠睡在裡面的樣子，比任何五星級貓窩都值得。

en.content:
You spent forty bucks
on a five-star cat bed.
Plush, heated, with a little canopy,
four-point-eight stars in the reviews.

The cat sniffs it once
and walks away.

Then it climbs into
the cardboard box
the bed was shipped in.

And sleeps like royalty.
en.editorNote: Send to the owner who just bought a fancy cat bed only to watch the cat adopt the box it came in — the oldest curse in cat ownership: they never use what you buy, only what it came wrapped in
variations: Advanced version: eventually you learn. You stop buying beds and just keep the boxes. The cat is thrilled. So is your wallet.
Sweet version: you grumble about the wasted money, but you keep that box anyway — because the way it sleeps curled up inside is worth more than any five-star bed.

items.sourceNote: 貓咪計算出最礙事的位置坐下的行為觀察，改編自 Pam Brown 名言概念，原創主子語錄

zh-tw.content:
主子擁有一項，
人類至今無法破解的天賦：

牠能精準計算出，
整個房間裡，
最礙事的那一個點，
然後，坐下。

你的鍵盤。
你正在看的書。
你剛攤開的瑜伽墊。
你要走過去的那條路中央。

從不失手。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個正在用手機打字、因為鍵盤被主子佔領的養貓人——牠不是來陪你的，牠是來行使否決權的
variations: 進階版：你移到沙發另一頭。三秒後，牠也移過來，重新坐回你的視線正中央。這不是巧合，這是領土宣示。
甜版：牠每次都坐在最礙事的地方，但你發現，那個最礙事的地方，永遠是離你最近的地方。

en.content:
The cat possesses a gift
no human has ever cracked:

it can calculate, with perfect precision,
the single most inconvenient spot
in the entire room,
and sit on it.

Your keyboard.
The book you're reading.
The yoga mat you just unrolled.
The exact center of the path you need to walk.

Never misses.
en.editorNote: Send to the owner typing on their phone because the cat has claimed the keyboard — it didn't come to keep you company, it came to exercise its veto power
variations: Advanced version: you move to the other end of the couch. Three seconds later, it moves too, resettling dead center in your line of sight. Not a coincidence. A territorial claim.
Sweet version: it always sits in the most inconvenient spot, but you've noticed the most inconvenient spot is always the one closest to you.

items.sourceNote: 貓咪凌晨三點暴衝（zoomies）、白天睡整天的作息對比，養貓人共同經驗，原創主子語錄

zh-tw.content:
主子的一天：

早上九點：睡。
中午十二點：睡。
下午三點：睡。
傍晚六點：睡。
晚上十點：睡。

凌晨三點：

變身。

以時速六十公里，
從客廳衝進房間，
再從房間衝回客廳，
撞翻一個垃圾桶，
踩過你的臉。

精力，從何而來，
無人知曉。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個被主子凌晨三點暴衝吵醒、踩過臉的養貓人——牠睡了一整天，就是為了這場深夜的個人演唱會
variations: 進階版：你研究過。牠白天累積的能量，全部濃縮在凌晨三點，一次釋放。這在物理學上，叫做「貓咪核融合」。
甜版：牠半夜暴衝吵醒你，但衝完之後，牠會跳上床，蜷在你腳邊呼嚕入睡。彷彿剛剛那場狂奔，只是為了確認，你還在。

en.content:
A cat's day:

9 a.m.: sleeping.
Noon: sleeping.
3 p.m.: sleeping.
6 p.m.: sleeping.
10 p.m.: sleeping.

3 a.m.:

Transformation.

Forty miles an hour,
living room to bedroom,
bedroom back to living room,
knocking over a trash can,
running across your face.

Where the energy comes from,
no one knows.
en.editorNote: Send to the owner jolted awake at 3 a.m. by zoomies and a paw to the face — it slept all day for exactly this, its private midnight concert
variations: Advanced version: you've studied it. All the energy banked during the day gets compressed into one 3 a.m. release. In physics this is known as cat fusion.

Sweet version: it wakes you with its midnight sprint, but the moment it's done it leaps onto the bed, curls at your feet, and purrs itself to sleep — as if the whole race was just to confirm you're still there.

items.sourceNote: 貓咪愛意表達方式——把東西推下桌、咬一口、半夜踩肚子，養貓人對「貓式愛語」的觀察，原創主子語錄

zh-tw.content:
貓咪的愛語，
跟人類不一樣。

牠不會說「我愛你」。

牠會：
把你的水杯推下桌。
在你睡著時踩你的肚子。
咬你一口，然後跑掉。
背對著你坐下。

你以為牠不在乎。

但牠選擇待在，
整個家裡，
離你最近的那個角落。

這，就是牠的方式。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個養了一隻傲嬌貓、常常懷疑牠到底愛不愛自己的養貓人——背對你坐下，是因為牠把背後交給了牠最信任的人
variations: 進階版：人類用擁抱表達愛，貓咪用「我願意在你面前露出肚子」表達愛。雖然你一摸牠就咬你，但那一秒的信任，是真的。
甜版：牠從不主動討抱，但每天你回家開門的那一刻，牠都已經坐在玄關等你了。牠不說，但牠一直都在等。

en.content:
A cat's love language
isn't like a human's.

It won't say "I love you."

Instead it will:
nudge your glass off the table,
stand on your stomach while you sleep,
bite you once, then run,
sit with its back turned to you.

You think it doesn't care.

But of every corner
in the whole house,
it chooses the one
closest to you.

That's its way.
en.editorNote: Send to the owner of an aloof cat who quietly wonders whether it loves them back — it sits with its back to you because it trusts you to guard it
variations: Advanced version: humans say love with a hug; cats say it with "I'll show you my belly." Sure, you touch it and you get bitten — but that one second of trust was real.
Sweet version: it never asks to be held, but every day the moment you open the door it's already sitting in the entryway, waiting. It won't say it, but it was always waiting.

items.sourceNote: 「養貓後手機相簿幾乎全是貓」的養貓人共通經驗，原創改寫為主子的相簿佔有率宣告

zh-tw.content:
養貓前，
手機相簿裡有：
風景、美食、朋友、自拍。

養貓後，
手機相簿裡有：

牠睡覺。
牠睡覺，但換了個角度。
牠睡覺，這次有露出一隻腳。
牠在看窗外。
牠在看窗外，但耳朵動了。

四千張照片，
同一隻貓，
零張你自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個手機容量永遠不夠、相簿翻到底全是同一隻貓的養貓人——他會邊笑邊點頭，因為他昨天才又拍了兩百張幾乎一樣的照片
variations: 進階版：你發誓要清理相簿空間，打開相簿，看到牠睡成一團的照片，於是你刪掉了三張自己的自拍，留下了那一百張貓。
甜版：你滑著那四千張幾乎一樣的照片，突然發現——你記得每一張是哪一天拍的。原來你不是在拍貓，你是在偷偷記錄，牠陪你的每一天。

en.content:
Before the cat,
my camera roll had:
scenery, food, friends, selfies.

After the cat,
my camera roll has:

it sleeping.
it sleeping, different angle.
it sleeping, one paw out this time.
it looking out the window.
it looking out the window, ear twitched.

Four thousand photos,
one cat,
zero of me.
en.editorNote: Send to the owner whose phone is permanently out of storage and whose camera roll is just one cat to the horizon — they'll laugh and nod, having shot two hundred near-identical photos just yesterday
variations: Advanced version: you swear you'll free up space, open the gallery, see it curled into a loaf, and end up deleting three of your own selfies to keep a hundred of the cat.

Sweet version: scrolling those four thousand near-identical photos, you realize you remember the exact day behind every one. You weren't photographing a cat — you were quietly logging every day it spent with you.

items.sourceNote: 「成貓之間幾乎不喵叫，喵叫是貓專門為了操控人類發明的語言」的行為學觀察，原創改寫為主子的語言起源論

zh-tw.content:
冷知識：

成年的貓，
對其他貓，
幾乎不喵叫。

「喵」這個語言，
是牠們專門，
為了人類，
發明的。

也就是說，
牠每天對你喵的那幾百聲，
不是在說話，

是在下指令。

而你，
每一次都照做了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個一聽到主子喵就自動起身開罐罐、開門、讓位的養貓人——你以為你聽懂了貓話，其實是牠成功訓練了你
variations: 進階版：你研究過貓的叫聲分十六種。錯了。是十六種指令。「喂食」「開門」「摸我」「不要摸我」「為什麼還沒喂食」⋯⋯每一種你都背得滾瓜爛熟。
甜版：牠對全世界都高冷不出聲，唯獨對你，整天喵個不停。那不是噪音，那是牠只想對你一個人說的、全世界最囉嗦的情話。

en.content:
Fun fact:

Adult cats
barely meow
at other cats.

The language "meow"
was invented
by cats,
specifically
for humans.

Which means
the few hundred meows
it aims at you each day
aren't conversation.

They're commands.

And you?
You obey
every single one.
en.editorNote: Send to the owner who springs up to crack a can, open a door, or surrender a seat the instant the cat meows — you think you learned cat; really, the cat trained you
variations: Advanced version: you've read that cats have sixteen distinct vocalizations. Wrong. Sixteen distinct commands. "Feed me," "open this," "pet me," "stop petting me," "why am I not yet fed" — and you've memorized all of them.
Sweet version: it's silent and aloof with the entire world, except you — to you it chatters all day long. That's not noise. That's the world's most demanding love note, addressed to you alone.

items.sourceNote: 「貓咪一天睡十六小時卻永遠一臉疲憊」的養貓人共通觀察，原創改寫為主子的專業睡眠履歷

zh-tw.content:
主子的履歷表：

職稱：專業睡眠師。
年資：一輩子。
每日工時：十六小時起跳。
專長：在任何表面、任何姿勢、任何時刻入睡。

備註：
儘管擁有如此豐富的睡眠經驗，
本人仍隨時保持一臉，
「我今天真的好累」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個自己加班到半夜、回家卻看到主子睡了一整天還打哈欠喊累的養貓人——牠的疲憊毫無根據，但牠的演技無懈可擊
variations: 進階版：你算過。牠一天睡十六小時，等於人生有三分之二在睡。而那剩下的三分之一，牠大部分時間在思考要不要去睡。
甜版：牠睡遍了全家所有舒服的地方，最貴的貓床、曬到太陽的窗台、你的棉被。但你發現，牠睡最久、最沉的那個地方，永遠是你的腿上。

en.content:
The cat's résumé:

Title: Professional Sleeper.
Experience: entire life.
Daily hours: sixteen and up.
Skills: falling asleep on any surface, in any pose, at any moment.

Note:
Despite this extensive sleep experience,
the applicant maintains, at all times,
the expression of someone who is
"honestly so tired today."
en.editorNote: Send to the owner who worked until midnight only to come home to a cat that slept all day and is now yawning about how exhausted it is — its fatigue has no basis, but its acting is flawless
variations: Advanced version: you did the math. Sixteen hours a day means two-thirds of its life is spent asleep. The other third, mostly, is spent deciding whether to go back to sleep.

Sweet version: it has slept in every comfortable spot in the house — the priciest cat bed, the sunny windowsill, your duvet. But you've noticed the spot where it sleeps longest and deepest is, always, your lap.

items.sourceNote: 「養貓後身上永遠有貓毛，尤其黑色衣服」的養貓人共通日常，原創改寫為主子的隨身配件宣言

zh-tw.content:
養貓人的穿搭守則：

你以為你今天穿的是純黑。
其實你穿的是，
黑底，撒上一層白毛。

你出門前用滾筒滾了五分鐘。
你關門前，牠在你腿上蹭了三秒。

結論：
貓毛不是髒。
貓毛是主子，
蓋在你身上的，
認證標章。

證明你，是有貓的人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個衣櫃裡再也沒有一件衣服是乾淨純色、出門前滾筒從不離手的養貓人——那層洗不掉的毛，是主子發給你的會員證
variations: 進階版：你已經放棄了。重要場合前你不再問「這件有沒有沾毛」，你改問「這件沾的毛跟我家貓的顏色搭不搭」。
甜版：有一天牠不在了，你打開衣櫃，發現每件衣服上都還沾著牠的毛。原來這些年，牠用最不起眼的方式，把自己留在了你身上。

en.content:
A cat owner's dress code:

You think you're wearing solid black.
You are actually wearing
black, dusted with a layer of white fur.

You lint-rolled for five minutes before leaving.
It rubbed against your leg for three seconds before you shut the door.

Conclusion:
cat hair isn't dirt.
Cat hair is the badge
the cat lays over you,

certifying that you
are a person who has a cat.
en.editorNote: Send to the owner whose closet no longer holds a single clean solid-color garment and who never leaves home without a lint roller — that un-removable layer of fur is the membership card the cat issued you
variations: Advanced version: you've given up. Before big occasions you no longer ask "is there fur on this?" You ask "does the fur on this match my cat's color?"
Sweet version: one day it's gone, and you open the closet to find its fur still clinging to every piece you own. All those years, in the quietest way possible, it had been leaving itself on you.

items.sourceNote: 「貓碗其實還有飼料，主子卻當成空碗在抗議」的養貓人共通日常，原創改寫為主子的飢荒申訴狀

zh-tw.content:
主子的飢荒申訴：

碗裡，明明還有飼料。
但那些飼料，
被我吃過、看過、踩過。

它們不再新鮮。
它們失去了，靈魂。

所以這個碗，
在情感上，
是空的。

請立刻為我加滿，
一模一樣的飼料。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個碗裡明明還有半碗飼料、卻被主子帶到碗邊一臉控訴喵到崩潰的養貓人——牠不是餓，牠是要新鮮現倒的儀式感
variations: 進階版：你倒了一模一樣的飼料進去。同一個牌子，同一個口味，同一個碗。牠看了一眼，立刻開始吃。問題從來不是飼料，是服務。
甜版：牠其實不是真的餓。牠只是想確認，當牠在碗邊喵喵叫的時候，你會放下手邊所有事情，走過來。對牠來說，那個「你會來」，比飼料重要。

en.content:
The cat's famine grievance:

There is, in fact, still food in the bowl.
But that food
has been eaten near, looked at, stepped on.

It is no longer fresh.
It has lost its soul.

Therefore this bowl
is, emotionally,
empty.

Please refill immediately
with the exact same food.
en.editorNote: Send to the owner whose cat leads them to a half-full bowl and yowls in pure accusation — it isn't hungry, it wants the ceremony of a fresh pour
variations: Advanced version: you pour in the exact same food. Same brand, same flavor, same bowl. It glances at it and starts eating immediately. The problem was never the food. It was the service.
Sweet version: it isn't actually hungry. It just wants to confirm that when it cries at the bowl, you'll drop everything and come. To the cat, the "you'll come" matters more than the food.

items.sourceNote: 「花大錢買貓窩，主子卻只愛紙箱」的養貓人共通崩潰，原創改寫為主子的選房宣言

zh-tw.content:
主子選房守則：

你買的貓床，三千塊。
進口記憶棉，恆溫設計。

我睡的地方，零塊。
你網購剛拆下來的，那個紙箱。

你問為什麼。

因為貓床，是你選的。
紙箱，是我選的。

在這個家，
選擇權，比舒適度，重要。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個砸錢買豪華貓窩、結果主子寧願擠在破紙箱裡的養貓人——牠不是不識貨，牠是要握有「我自己決定」的主導權
variations: 進階版：你後來學乖了。你不再買貓床。你直接把網購的紙箱留著，然後跟自己說，這三千塊省下來，是主子賞你的。
甜版：那個紙箱再小、再破，牠都要塞進去。後來你發現，牠選的從來不是箱子，是那個剛好放在你腳邊、看得到你的位置。

en.content:
The cat's housing rules:

The bed you bought: ninety dollars.
Imported memory foam, temperature-regulated.

Where I sleep: zero dollars.
The cardboard box your order just came in.

You ask why.

Because the bed was your choice.
The box was mine.

In this house,
the right to choose matters more
than comfort.
en.editorNote: Send to the owner who splurged on a luxury cat bed only for the cat to wedge itself into a beat-up box — it isn't clueless, it wants the power of "I decided this myself"
variations: Advanced version: you eventually wised up. You stopped buying beds. You just keep the shipping boxes now and tell yourself the ninety dollars you saved is a gift from the cat.
Sweet version: no matter how small or crushed the box, it'll cram itself in. Later you realize it was never choosing the box — it was choosing the spot right by your feet, where it can see you.

items.sourceNote: 「My cat's love is unconditional, unless I'm late with dinner」這類養貓人金句，原創改寫為主子的愛之條款

zh-tw.content:
主子的愛，是無條件的。

附帶以下條款：

一、晚餐準時。
二、罐罐每日一開。
三、本喵睡覺時，全屋禁止吸塵。
四、本喵坐下的地方，即為禁區。

違反任一條款，
無條件的愛，
將立即轉為，
無條件的不理你。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個以為養貓是被無條件愛著、結果發現條款比手機合約還多的養貓人——主子的愛是真的，但附則也是真的
variations: 進階版：你仔細想過。你跟主子的關係，比較像你跟電信業者。表面上是會員，實際上是綁約，而且續約的人永遠是你。
甜版：那些條款看起來很機車。但你發現，無論你違反幾條，當你半夜睡不著的時候，牠還是會默默走過來，蜷在你旁邊。有些愛，真的是無條件的。

en.content:
The cat's love is unconditional.

Subject to the following terms:

1. Dinner shall be on time.
2. One can shall be opened daily.
3. No vacuuming anywhere while the cat sleeps.
4. Wherever the cat sits becomes a restricted zone.

Breach of any term
shall immediately convert
unconditional love into
unconditional ignoring of you.
en.editorNote: Send to the owner who thought a cat would love them unconditionally and discovered the terms are longer than a phone contract — the love is real, but so is the fine print
variations: Advanced version: you've thought it through. Your relationship with the cat is basically your relationship with your carrier. On paper you're a member; really you're locked into a contract, and you're always the one who renews.

Sweet version: the terms look ruthless. But you've noticed that no matter how many you break, when you can't sleep at 3 a.m., it still quietly walks over and curls up beside you. Some love really is unconditional.

items.sourceNote: 「I pretend not to like you, but I sit on your keyboard for a reason」這類主子視角金句，原創改寫為主子的鍵盤宣言

zh-tw.content:
本喵的聲明：

我並不喜歡你。
我只是，剛好，
選擇坐在你最需要用的那塊鍵盤上。

我並不需要你。
我只是，剛好，
挑在你開視訊會議的那一刻，
從鏡頭前慢慢走過。

這些都是巧合。

但這麼多巧合，
全都剛好發生在你身上，
你自己想想，
是什麼意思。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個一打開筆電主子就準時來坐鍵盤、開會必被亂入的養貓人——牠嘴上說不在乎，但牠永遠選你，這就是貓的告白
variations: 進階版：你終於懂了。牠不是要坐鍵盤。牠是要坐「你正在看的東西」上面。對牠來說，你的注意力，必須是牠的。
甜版：牠從不撒嬌，從不主動討抱。但你工作的時候，牠一定來，擠到你跟螢幕中間。牠不會說我想你，牠只會用整個身體，擋住你，逼你看牠。

en.content:
An official statement from the cat:

I do not like you.
I merely happen
to choose the exact keys you most need.

I do not need you.
I merely happen
to stroll across the webcam
the moment your video call begins.

These are all coincidences.

But so many coincidences,
all happening to you specifically —
think about it,
and ask yourself what that means.
en.editorNote: Send to the owner whose cat lands on the keyboard the instant the laptop opens and photobombs every meeting — it claims not to care, but it always picks you, and that's a cat's confession
variations: Advanced version: you finally get it. It isn't trying to sit on the keyboard. It's trying to sit on whatever you're looking at. To the cat, your attention has to be its own.

Sweet version: it never begs for cuddles, never asks to be held. But the second you start working, it shows up and wedges itself between you and the screen. It won't say it misses you — it'll just use its whole body to block you, forcing you to look.

items.sourceNote: 「My cat's superpower: ignoring me with elegance」這類主子無視主人的金句，原創改寫為主子的無視藝術

zh-tw.content:
你叫牠的名字。
牠聽到了。
牠的耳朵轉了過來。

然後，
牠決定不理你。

這不是沒聽見。
這是一個經過深思熟慮的決定。

主子的超能力，
是用最優雅的姿態，
當你不存在。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個一直喊貓名字、貓卻連頭都不轉的養貓人——他會苦笑，因為他知道那隻耳朵明明動了
variations: 進階版：牠不只無視你，牠還會在無視你的同時，慢慢眨一下眼，好像在說「我知道你在叫我，而我選擇不回應，這就是我們之間的關係」。
甜版：但奇怪的是，等你不叫了、坐下了、放棄了，牠就會自己走過來，跳到你腿上。原來牠不是不理你，牠只是不喜歡被命令。

en.content:
You call its name.
It hears you.
One ear swivels toward you.

And then
it decides not to respond.

This is not a failure to hear.
This is a carefully considered decision.

A cat's superpower
is ignoring you
with total elegance.
en.editorNote: Send to the owner who calls the cat's name and gets nothing but a twitching ear — they know that ear moved, and they know exactly what it means
variations: Advanced version: it doesn't just ignore you. It ignores you while slowly blinking, as if to say, 'I know you're calling me, and I'm choosing not to answer. That's our whole relationship.'
Sweet version: but here's the strange part — the moment you stop calling, sit down, and give up, it walks over and hops into your lap. It wasn't ignoring you. It just hates being summoned.

items.sourceNote: 「Cats: the only creatures capable of sleeping 20 hours a day and still being tired」睡20小時還累的金句，原創改寫為主子的睡眠時間表

zh-tw.content:
主子的一天：
睡到中午。
醒來，吃飯。
累了，睡個午覺。
醒來，發呆。
為剛剛的發呆感到疲憊，再睡。
半夜暴衝二十分鐘。
累壞了，繼續睡。

全世界唯一一種，
一天睡二十小時，
還是覺得自己很忙的生物。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個總說「真希望我能像我家貓一樣過生活」的朋友——這就是他羨慕的人生，被精準翻譯出來了
variations: 進階版：而那剩下的四小時清醒時間，牠拿來做什麼？盯著你、要你開罐頭、然後嫌你開得太慢。
甜版：但你知道嗎，牠睡二十小時，其中一定有幾小時是貼著你睡的。牠可以睡在任何地方，但牠選了你身邊。

en.content:
A cat's daily schedule:
Sleep until noon.
Wake up, eat.
Feeling tired, take a nap.
Wake up, stare into space.
Feel exhausted from all that staring, sleep again.
Sprint around the house at 3 a.m. for twenty minutes.
Utterly drained, go back to sleep.

The only creature on Earth
that sleeps twenty hours a day
and still considers itself busy.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who always says 'I wish I could live like my cat' — here's the life they envy, translated with brutal accuracy
variations: Advanced version: and those four waking hours? Spent staring at you, demanding you open a can, then judging how slowly you open it.
Sweet version: but here's the thing — of those twenty hours, a few are always spent pressed against you. It could sleep anywhere. It chose your side.

items.sourceNote: 「Courage is knocking over a glass at 3am and walking away」推杯子後走人的金句，原創改寫為主子的犯罪現場

zh-tw.content:
凌晨三點。
一聲玻璃杯落地的脆響。

你衝出房間。
現場只剩碎片。

而主子，
已經坐在沙發另一頭，
用一種「我什麼都不知道」的表情，
優雅地舔著自己的腳掌。

真正的勇氣，
是把杯子推下桌，
然後從容地走開。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個半夜被「啪」一聲驚醒、衝出去只看到貓在裝無辜的養貓人——他會立刻想起昨晚的那只馬克杯
variations: 進階版：而且牠不是隨便推。牠會先用爪子確認杯子的位置，跟你對到眼，再慢慢、慢慢地，把它推到邊緣。這是一場有觀眾的表演。
甜版：你氣得半死，但你還是把碎片掃乾淨，沒有真的罵牠。因為你知道，明天牠又會推一次，而你還是會原諒牠。這大概就是愛。

en.content:
3 a.m.
The crisp shatter of glass hitting the floor.

You bolt out of your room.
Nothing left but fragments.

And the cat
is already sitting at the far end of the couch,
wearing a face that says 'I know nothing,'
gracefully licking its paw.

True courage
is knocking a glass off the table
and calmly walking away.
en.editorNote: Send to the owner who's been jolted awake by a 'crash' only to find the cat playing innocent — they'll picture last night's mug instantly
variations: Advanced version: and it doesn't knock things off carelessly. It checks the glass's position with a paw, locks eyes with you, then slowly — slowly — nudges it to the edge. It's a performance, and you are the audience.
Sweet version: you're furious, but you still sweep up the pieces without really yelling. Because you know it'll do it again tomorrow, and you'll forgive it again. That's probably love.

items.sourceNote: 「If I fits, I sits. If I don't, I force it」紙箱金句，原創改寫為主子與容器的物理學

zh-tw.content:
主子的物理學定律：

只要裝得下，就坐進去。
如果裝不下，
就硬擠進去。

紙箱、籃子、洗手台、
比牠小三號的玩具盒——
沒有牠塞不進去的容器，
只有牠還沒嘗試的角落。

你花八百塊買的貓窩，
牠看都不看。
你網購寄來的空紙箱，
是牠的宮殿。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個買了昂貴貓窩、貓卻只睡紙箱的養貓人——他的錢包和他的心，都被主子的審美狠狠教育過
variations: 進階版：而且箱子越小，牠越要進去。牠會把自己折疊成一個你以為不可能的形狀，肉從四面八方溢出來，然後露出一臉「這裡剛剛好」的滿足。
甜版：其實牠不是愛紙箱，牠是愛那種被包圍、被保護的安全感。下次牠擠進你的懷裡時，你就懂了——你，就是牠最喜歡的那個箱子。

en.content:
A cat's law of physics:

If it fits, it sits.
If it doesn't fit,
it forces it.

Boxes, baskets, the bathroom sink,
a toy bin three sizes too small —
there is no container it can't squeeze into,
only corners it hasn't tried yet.

The fancy cat bed you spent a fortune on?
It won't even glance at it.
The empty box your package came in?
That's its palace.
en.editorNote: Send to the owner who bought a pricey cat bed only for the cat to sleep in the cardboard box — their wallet and their pride have both been schooled by feline taste
variations: Advanced version: and the smaller the box, the more it insists. It folds itself into a shape you'd swear was impossible, fluff spilling out every side, wearing a look of pure 'this fits perfectly' contentment.
Sweet version: it doesn't actually love boxes — it loves the feeling of being enclosed and safe. Next time it squeezes into your arms, you'll understand: you are its favorite box.

items.sourceNote: 「My cat's love language is knocking things off tables / I used to have control over my life. Then I got a cat」失去人生控制權金句，原創改寫為主子接管家政

zh-tw.content:
養貓前，
我以為這是我的家。

養貓後我才明白：

這是牠的家。
沙發是牠的。
床的正中央是牠的。
我的鍵盤是牠的。
陽光灑進來的那塊地板，當然也是牠的。

我只是那個，
付房租、開罐頭、
還要負責道歉的，
人類室友。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每一個自稱「鏟屎官」的人——標題說是「我養貓」，但實際上是誰養誰，大家心知肚明
variations: 進階版：而且這個室友沒有投票權。家裡所有決定，包括幾點該起床餵飯、哪個紙箱不能丟，都是牠說了算。
甜版：但說真的，就算把整個家都讓給牠，你也甘願。因為每天晚上牠窩進你懷裡呼嚕的那一刻，你就覺得，這場交易，你賺翻了。

en.content:
Before I got a cat,
I thought this was my home.

After I got a cat, I understood:

This is its home.
The couch is its.
The exact center of the bed is its.
My keyboard is its.
And that patch of floor where the sun lands? Obviously its.

I'm just
the human roommate
who pays the rent, opens the cans,
and is also in charge of apologizing.
en.editorNote: Send to everyone who calls themselves a 'cat servant' — the title says 'I have a cat,' but everyone knows exactly who owns whom
variations: Advanced version: and this roommate gets no vote. Every household decision — what time you wake to feed it, which box may never be thrown out — is made unilaterally by the cat.
Sweet version: but honestly, you'd hand over the whole house gladly. Because the moment it curls into your arms and starts purring at night, you feel like you got the better end of the deal.

items.sourceNote: Cat love language joke about knocking things off tables; rewritten with original framing

zh-tw.content:
我家貓的愛的語言，
是把東西從桌上推下去。

而且牠不是隨便推。
牠會先用爪子輕輕碰一下，
確認那是你最在乎的東西，
再死死盯著你的眼睛，
慢——慢——把它撥到地上。

那不是調皮，
那是宣示主權：
「這張桌子是我的，重力也是我的。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 貼給家裡杯子馬克筆遙控器都被掃落過的貓奴，配一張現場掉落圖更有感
variations: 進階版：而且牠推完還會回頭看你一眼，那眼神彷彿在說「下次記得放回去，我還要再推。」
甜版：但說真的，被牠掃下去的東西再貴，也比不上牠願意花時間在你桌上搗亂——那代表牠把你的地盤當成自己的家。

en.content:
My cat's love language
is knocking things off the table.

And it's not random.
First it taps the object with one paw
to confirm it's the thing you care about most,
then it locks eyes with you
and slooowly
nudges it to the floor.

That's not mischief.
That's a statement of sovereignty:
"This table is mine. So is gravity."
en.editorNote: For anyone whose mugs, pens, and remotes have all been swept to the floor — pair it with a photo mid-fall for full effect
variations: Advanced version: and after it pushes, it looks back at you with an expression that says 'put it back, I want to do that again.'
Sweet version: but honestly, whatever it knocks down is worth less than the fact that it chose your desk to wreck — that means it claims your space as its own home.

items.sourceNote: 4am food-demand cat humor; rewritten and localized

zh-tw.content:
我家貓平常很安靜，
一整天頂多喵兩三聲。

但只要時間一到清晨五點，
牠就會準時開機，
變成一隻有口音的鬧鐘：

喵——（飯）
喵喵——（現在）
喵喵喵——（我說現在）

而且這個鬧鐘
沒有貪睡鍵，
關掉的唯一方法
是起來開罐頭。
zh-tw.editorNote: 貼給每天被貓準時鬧醒的人，標題可以配「我家有一台不能關的鬧鐘」
variations: 進階版：最可怕的是牠連假日都不放過。你想睡到中午？牠的胃可不知道什麼叫週末。
甜版：但其實牠不是只想要飯，牠是想確認你還在。每天五點那聲喵，是牠在說『早安，今天也要好好活著喔』。

en.content:
My cat is usually quiet —
two, maybe three meows a whole day.

But the second the clock hits 5 a.m.,
it boots up right on schedule
and turns into an alarm clock with an accent:

Meow — (food)
Meow meow — (now)
Meow meow meow — (I said NOW)

And this alarm
has no snooze button.
The only way to turn it off
is to get up and open a can.
en.editorNote: For everyone woken at dawn on the dot — caption it 'I own an alarm clock that can't be switched off'
variations: Advanced version: the scary part is it doesn't take weekends off. You wanted to sleep till noon? Its stomach has never heard of Saturday.
Sweet version: but it doesn't really just want food — it wants to confirm you're still here. That 5 a.m. meow is its way of saying 'good morning, let's both make it through today.'

items.sourceNote: Cat ranking/ignoring owner humor; rewritten with original elaboration

zh-tw.content:
貓不是在無視你，
牠只是把你排得比較後面。

在牠的優先順序裡：
第一名是窗外那隻鳥，
第二名是地上那道陽光，
第三名是那個你買貴的紙箱，
第四名是牠自己的尾巴，

而你，
大概排在「會開罐頭的那台機器」
上面一點點而已。
zh-tw.editorNote: 貼給叫貓十聲牠連耳朵都不動一下的人，自嘲用最剛好
variations: 進階版：但只要你拿出零食罐，你的排名就會瞬間衝到第一。原來感情是可以用罐罐買的。
甜版：可是每次你難過縮在沙發上，那個平常無視你的傢伙，會默默走過來坐在你旁邊。原來牠的排名，是分場合的。

en.content:
Your cat isn't ignoring you.
It just ranks you fairly low.

On its priority list:
Number one is the bird outside the window,
number two is that patch of sunlight on the floor,
number three is the box you overpaid for,
number four is its own tail,

and you
sit roughly one slot above
'the machine that opens cans.'
en.editorNote: For anyone who's called their cat ten times and gotten not even an ear twitch — perfect for self-deprecating captions
variations: Advanced version: but the moment you grab the treat jar, your ranking rockets straight to number one. Turns out affection is purchasable, by the pouch.
Sweet version: yet whenever you're curled sad on the couch, that same aloof creature quietly walks over and sits beside you. Turns out its rankings depend on the occasion.

items.sourceNote: Cat-on-laptop 'your warm laptop is mine now' humor; rewritten

zh-tw.content:
我以為筆電是我的，
直到我家貓提出異議。

牠的邏輯很簡單：
那台機器會發熱，
會發出你很在意的聲音，
還會讓你坐著不動好幾個小時——

所以牠合理推斷，
這一定是個很重要、很舒服的位子，
而那個位子
理應屬於牠。

於是現在我打字
都得繞過一隻貓。
zh-tw.editorNote: 貼給每個遠端工作、鍵盤永遠被貓佔據的人，配一張貓趴鍵盤圖剛剛好
variations: 進階版：而且牠很會挑時機，永遠在你開會、要交件、滑鼠正要點下去的那一秒坐上來。
甜版：但說真的，每次牠暖暖地趴在我手邊呼嚕，我反而打字打得更慢，因為捨不得把牠移開——原來最難交的件，是離開一隻貓。

en.content:
I thought the laptop was mine,
until my cat filed an objection.

Its logic is simple:
that machine gives off heat,
makes the sounds you clearly care about,
and keeps you sitting still for hours —

so it reasonably concludes
this must be an important, comfortable spot,
and that spot
rightfully belongs to it.

Now I type
by reaching around a cat.
en.editorNote: For every remote worker whose keyboard is permanently occupied — pair with a photo of a cat sprawled across the keys
variations: Advanced version: and its timing is impeccable — it lands the exact second you join a meeting, hit a deadline, or move the mouse to click 'send.'
Sweet version: but honestly, when it's curled warm by my hand and purring, I type slower on purpose, because I can't bear to move it — turns out the hardest deadline is leaving a cat.

items.sourceNote: 'My cat judges me constantly' staring humor; rewritten with original spin

zh-tw.content:
我家貓沒有在發呆。

牠是坐在房間角落，
用一種看透一切的眼神，
安靜地審判我。

牠看著我吃第三塊餅乾，
看著我又把要洗的衣服堆成山，
看著我跟手機講話講到半夜，

什麼都不說，
只是緩緩眨了一下眼。

那一眨，
比任何一句念我的話
都還要重。
zh-tw.editorNote: 貼給總覺得自己被貓默默評分的人，自嘲生活廢度時很好用
variations: 進階版：最可怕的是牠從來不給評語，只給表情。你永遠不知道自己這次又掉了幾分。
甜版：但牠審判歸審判，到了晚上還是會來窩在我腳邊。原來牠不是嫌棄我，牠只是用牠的方式，看著我把日子過下去。

en.content:
My cat isn't just zoning out.

It's sitting in the corner of the room,
wearing a look that sees straight through me,
quietly passing judgment.

It watches me eat a third cookie,
watches me let the laundry pile into a mountain,
watches me talk to my phone past midnight,

says nothing,
and just slowly blinks once.

That single blink
weighs more
than any lecture ever could.
en.editorNote: For anyone who feels silently scored by their cat — great for self-deprecating posts about your life choices
variations: Advanced version: the worst part is it never gives notes, only expressions. You never find out how many points you just lost.
Sweet version: but judgment aside, come nightfall it still curls up by my feet. Turns out it isn't disappointed in me — it's just watching, its own way, as I make it through another day.

items.sourceNote: 'My cat runs the house, I just pay the bills' control humor; rewritten with original framing

zh-tw.content:
我家不是我在管的。

主子決定幾點開飯，
主子決定哪張椅子是牠的，
主子決定我今天能不能好好睡。

我只負責一件事——
按時繳水電費、租金、罐罐錢。

說穿了，
我不是屋主，
我是這個家的金主兼工讀生。
zh-tw.editorNote: 貼給每個自稱「家裡主導權在貓手上」的奴才，認領自己的金主身分時很有共鳴
variations: 進階版：連我自己的床，都是主子睡剩下的角落分我躺的。
甜版：但每個月帳單繳下去，看到牠睡得四腳朝天那麼安心，又覺得這份工讀，做得心甘情願。

en.content:
I'm not the one running this house.

The cat decides when dinner is served,
the cat decides which chair belongs to it,
the cat decides whether I sleep well tonight.

My one job?
Pay the rent, the utilities, and the endless can supply on time.

Let's be honest:
I'm not the homeowner.
I'm the household's investor and part-time intern.
en.editorNote: For every servant who jokes that the cat is in charge — claim your title as full-time funder
variations: Advanced version: even my own bed is just the corner the cat hasn't claimed yet.
Sweet version: but every month when the bills clear and I see it sleeping belly-up without a care, this internship suddenly feels completely worth it.

items.sourceNote: 'My cat's love is unconditional, unless I'm late with dinner' food-demand humor; rewritten

zh-tw.content:
主子對我的愛，是無條件的。

除了——
開飯晚了五分鐘的時候。

那五分鐘裡，
我從牠最愛的人，
降級成牠世界上最大的仇人。

罐頭一打開，
仇恨瞬間蒸發，
我又變回那個牠願意磨蹭一下的人。

愛情，
原來真的可以用一個罐罐衡量。
zh-tw.editorNote: 貼給每個被貓的飯點支配人生的人，自嘲「我的地位等於罐頭」時超好笑
variations: 進階版：晚開飯的下場是——牠會坐在你面前，用一種「我餓死之後第一個托夢的就是你」的眼神盯著你。
甜版：其實牠不是只愛罐罐。只是牠不會說話，只好用『催飯』這件事，提醒你牠還在等你回家。

en.content:
My cat's love for me is unconditional.

Except—
when dinner is five minutes late.

In those five minutes,
I go from its favorite human
to its sworn nemesis in the entire universe.

The second the can pops open,
the grudge evaporates,
and I'm back to being the one it deigns to rub against.

Turns out love really can
be measured in a single can of food.
en.editorNote: For anyone whose whole life is ruled by feeding time — perfect for joking that your worth equals one can of food
variations: Advanced version: the punishment for a late meal is the stare — the one that says 'when I starve to death, you'll be the first ghost I visit.'
Sweet version: it doesn't really just love the food. It just can't talk, so it uses the dinner countdown to remind you it's been waiting for you to come home.

items.sourceNote: 'I'm not ignoring you, I'm prioritizing my nap schedule' ignoring humor; rewritten

zh-tw.content:
我沒有在無視你。

我只是把睡覺這件事，
排在你前面而已。

還有吃飯、舔毛、發呆、
看窗外的鳥、踩奶、
以及對著空氣放空——

這些全部，
都排在你前面。

別誤會，
你還是有在我的待辦清單裡。
只是排在很後面而已。
zh-tw.editorNote: 用貓的口吻寫的無視宣言，貼給每個被自家貓已讀不回的奴才
variations: 進階版：你叫我名字的時候，我有聽到。我只是選擇用『動一下耳朵』來回覆你，這已經是我最大的誠意了。
甜版：不過如果你真的難過，我會在你不注意的時候，悄悄走過來，坐在離你一步的地方。不是靠近，但也沒有走遠。

en.content:
I'm not ignoring you.

I've simply ranked sleeping
slightly above you.

Also eating, grooming, zoning out,
bird-watching, making biscuits,
and staring blankly into empty air—

all of those
rank above you too.

Don't take it the wrong way,
you're still on my to-do list.
Just very, very far down it.
en.editorNote: An ignoring manifesto written from the cat's point of view — for every servant left on read by their own cat
variations: Advanced version: I heard you call my name. I chose to reply with a single ear twitch. That's the most sincerity you're getting today.
Sweet version: but if you're truly upset, I'll quietly wander over when you're not looking and sit one step away. Not close, but not far either.

items.sourceNote: 'Cats don't have owners, they have staff' service humor; rewritten with job-description spin

zh-tw.content:
貓沒有主人，
貓只有員工。

我的職務內容包括：
鏟屎專員、開罐技師、
人體暖暖包、移動式抓抓板、
以及半夜陪玩值班人員。

年資三年，
零升遷、零加薪、
年終獎金是被咬一口。

但奇怪的是，
我從來沒想過離職。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把養貓寫成一份職缺說明，貼給每個自嘲『我是貓的員工』的奴才同事
variations: 進階版：這份工作最特別的地方是——考核標準從來沒人告訴你，你只能從牠的眼神，猜自己這季的表現。
甜版：說是員工，但每當牠主動跳上來窩在我身上，我就知道，這份工作給的薪水，是用別的方式發的。

en.content:
Cats don't have owners.
Cats have staff.

My job description includes:
litter technician, can-opening specialist,
human heating pad, mobile scratching post,
and on-call midnight playmate.

Three years of service,
zero promotions, zero raises,
and my annual bonus is a single bite.

Yet somehow,
I've never once thought about quitting.
en.editorNote: Cat ownership written as a job posting — for every coworker who jokes that they work for their cat
variations: Advanced version: the strangest part of this job is that nobody ever tells you the performance criteria. You just read its eyes and guess how you did this quarter.
Sweet version: staff, sure — but every time it climbs up and curls onto me on its own, I realize this job pays its salary in a different currency.

items.sourceNote: Cat sitting on keyboard to 'help' meme; rewritten as a remote-work co-worker bit

zh-tw.content:
你以為我是來搗亂的。

不。
我是來開會的。

我坐在鍵盤上，
是因為這份報告，
需要有貓的角度。

而現在，
你的滑鼠歸我管。
你的進度，
也由我決定。
zh-tw.editorNote: 在家工作被貓佔領鍵盤時可以貼這則，傳給每個視訊會議都有貓亂入的遠端工作者
variations: 進階版：我不只是坐在鍵盤上，我是在替你按下『今天到此為止』這個快捷鍵。
甜版：而且我選的位置剛好擋住螢幕——這樣你才會看我，不會一直看工作。

en.content:
You think I'm here to cause trouble.

No.
I'm here for the meeting.

I'm sitting on your keyboard
because this report
clearly needs a cat's perspective.

And as of now,
your mouse is mine.
Your deadline
is also my decision.
en.editorNote: Post this when your cat takes over the keyboard during work-from-home — for every remote worker whose video calls get crashed by a cat
variations: Advanced version: I'm not just sitting on the keyboard. I'm pressing the shortcut for 'that's enough work for today.'
Sweet version: and I picked the spot that blocks the screen on purpose — so you'll look at me instead of your job.

items.sourceNote: 'There are many intelligent species, all owned by cats' aphorism; rewritten as a cosmic ranking

zh-tw.content:
宇宙裡有許多聰明的物種。

而牠們，
全部都歸貓管。

人類發明了火、文字、火箭，
最後的成就，
是學會了怎麼正確地開罐頭。

別爭了，
食物鏈的頂端
從來都不是力氣最大的，
而是叫一聲，
你就會起床的那位。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合配貓咪睥睨眾生的照片，貼給每個自嘲被貓統治的奴才
variations: 進階版：人類以為自己是在養貓。其實是貓，決定讓你參與牠的生活。
甜版：但牠願意把宇宙第一的位置，留一小塊給你——就在牠睡覺時，挑你的胸口當枕頭那一刻。

en.content:
There are many intelligent species in the universe.

And every single one of them
is owned by cats.

Humans invented fire, writing, rockets—
and their crowning achievement
was learning to open a can correctly.

Stop arguing.
The top of the food chain
was never the strongest one.
It's whoever can meow once
and make you get out of bed.
en.editorNote: Pairs well with a photo of a cat looking down on all of creation — for every human who jokes about being ruled by their cat
variations: Advanced version: humans think they own a cat. In reality, the cat decided to let you take part in its life.
Sweet version: but it does save one small corner of its number-one spot for you — the moment it picks your chest as a pillow.

items.sourceNote: Cleveland Amory 'cats have enormous patience with humankind' quote; rewritten as a patient-teacher bit

zh-tw.content:
別擔心，
我對你的笨，
有非常大的耐心。

你叫錯我的名字，
我原諒你。
你倒飼料倒太慢，
我也忍下來了。
你抱我的姿勢每次都不對，
我還是讓你抱。

當一隻貓的奴才，
是需要從頭教起的。
所幸，
我有的是時間。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合配貓咪一臉無奈看主人的照片，貼給每個被貓嫌棄卻甘之如飴的奴才
variations: 進階版：我不是在生氣，我只是在等你自己想通，剛剛那個動作哪裡讓我不滿意。
甜版：其實你做的每件事我都記得。包括那些做對的——只是我選擇用呼嚕聲，而不是話語，告訴你。

en.content:
Don't worry.
I have enormous patience
for how clueless you are.

You say my name wrong—
I forgive you.
You pour the food too slowly—
I endure it.
You hold me wrong every single time—
and still I let you.

Being a cat's servant
has to be taught from scratch.
Luckily for you,
time is the one thing I have.
en.editorNote: Pairs well with a cat giving its owner a long-suffering stare — for every human their cat tolerates and adores in equal measure
variations: Advanced version: I'm not angry. I'm just waiting for you to figure out, on your own, what you did wrong just now.
Sweet version: actually, I remember everything you do — including the things you get right. I just tell you with a purr instead of words.

items.sourceNote: 'Testing gravity one paw at a time' knock-things-off joke; rewritten as a deadpan science-experiment log

zh-tw.content:
我不是在搗蛋。
我是在做實驗。

第一天：把杯子推下桌。掉了。
第二天：把筆推下桌。掉了。
第三天：把你的眼鏡推下桌。掉了。

結論：
地心引力依然穩定運作，
樣本數充足，誤差極小。

附註：
人類在每次實驗後
都會發出奇怪的尖叫聲，
此現象，尚待進一步研究。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合配貓咪盯著桌緣物品的照片，貼給每個家裡東西常常莫名其妙掉滿地的養貓人
variations: 進階版：為求嚴謹，我會看著你的眼睛再推一次——這叫對照組。
甜版：唯一一個我從來不推下桌的東西，是放著你味道的那件外套。它得留在我看得到的地方。

en.content:
I'm not being naughty.
I'm running an experiment.

Day 1: pushed a cup off the table. It fell.
Day 2: pushed a pen off the table. It fell.
Day 3: pushed your glasses off the table. They fell.

Conclusion:
gravity remains stable and reliable,
sample size sufficient, margin of error minimal.

Footnote:
the human emits a strange shriek
after each trial.
This phenomenon requires further study.
en.editorNote: Pairs well with a cat eyeing an object at the table's edge — for every owner who keeps finding their stuff on the floor for no clear reason
variations: Advanced version: for scientific rigor, I'll make eye contact and push it again. We call that the control group.
Sweet version: the one thing I never push off the table is the jacket that smells like you. That has to stay where I can see it.

items.sourceNote: 貓咪無視昂貴貓床、偏愛紙箱的經典行為，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
你花了兩千塊買了一張記憶棉貓床。

本喵的評估結果：
床——不行。
那個裝床的紙箱——完美。

錢是你的事，品味是我的事。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給剛幫貓買了高級用品、結果貓只玩箱子的朋友——附上一張貓塞在小紙箱裡的照片效果最好
variations: 進階版：那張床我會去躺，但只在你拍照拍累了、放棄了之後。
土味版：唯一能讓我離開紙箱的，是你坐下來的那個位置——因為那裡有你。

en.content:
You spent sixty bucks on a memory-foam cat bed.

My official review:
The bed — no.
The box the bed came in — perfect.

Your money is your business. My taste is mine.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend who just bought their cat something fancy and watched it play with the box instead — works best with a photo of a cat wedged into a tiny box
variations: Advanced version: I will lie on the bed, but only after you've given up taking photos of me on it.
Sweet version: the only thing that gets me out of the box is the spot you just sat down in. That one has you in it.

items.sourceNote: 貓咪總在飼主忙碌時索討關注，原創主子語錄

zh-tw.content:
本喵的關愛排程：
你睡覺時——清醒。
你工作時——需要抱抱。
你想抱我時——消失。

時機就是一切。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給遠端工作、每次開會貓就跳上鍵盤的朋友——這是全天下貓奴的共同命運
variations: 進階版：我能精準算出你最不方便的那一秒，然後在那一秒坐到你的筆電上。
土味版：我挑你最忙的時候討抱，不是故意的——是因為我也只有那時候最想你。

en.content:
My affection schedule:
While you sleep — wide awake.
While you work — need cuddles now.
When you want to hold me — gone.

Timing is everything.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend who works from home and gets a cat on the keyboard every single meeting — it's the universal fate of cat owners
variations: Advanced version: I can calculate the single least convenient second of your day, and that is when I sit on your laptop.
Sweet version: I ask for cuddles when you're busiest not to annoy you — it's just the only time I miss you the most.

items.sourceNote: 「狗有主人，貓有員工」概念改寫，避免直接引用

zh-tw.content:
釐清一下我們的關係：
你不是我的主人。
你是我的助理。

你負責罐頭、清貓砂、開門。
我負責被欣賞。

分工很清楚。
zh-tw.editorNote: 養貓人自嘲必備——傳到貓奴群組，每個人都會回「對，就是這樣」
variations: 進階版：你以為你養了一隻貓，其實是一隻貓僱用了你，而且沒付薪水。
土味版：說是助理啦，但你下班晚回家我會擔心——這種助理，我捨不得換掉的是你。

en.content:
Let's clarify our relationship:
You are not my owner.
You are my assistant.

You handle the cans, the litter box, and the doors.
I handle being admired.

Clear division of labor.
en.editorNote: Essential cat-owner self-deprecation — drop it in the group chat and everyone replies 'yep, that's it exactly'
variations: Advanced version: you think you adopted a cat. Actually, a cat hired you — and never put you on payroll.
Sweet version: I call you my assistant, but I worry when you come home late. This assistant I'd never replace.

items.sourceNote: 貓咪三更半夜暴衝（the zoomies）行為，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
凌晨三點作戰計畫：

從走廊全速衝刺。
撞翻一個東西。
對著牆壁大叫。
回去睡覺，彷彿什麼都沒發生。

本喵不需要解釋。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給被半夜貓咪暴衝吵醒、頂著黑眼圈的朋友——附一句「我懂」就夠了
variations: 進階版：你問我為什麼凌晨三點要這樣？因為那是我的時間。白天是你的，深夜是我的。
土味版：其實我半夜衝來衝去，最後都是停在你的床邊——確認你還在，我才睡得著。

en.content:
3 a.m. battle plan:

Sprint full speed down the hallway.
Knock one thing over.
Yell at a wall.
Go back to sleep like nothing happened.

I owe you no explanation.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend with dark circles from a cat's midnight zoomies — just add 'I feel you' and you're done
variations: Advanced version: you ask why I do this at 3 a.m.? Because that's my shift. Daytime is yours. The night is mine.
Sweet version: all that midnight sprinting always ends at your bedside. I check you're still there, then I can sleep.

items.sourceNote: 貓咪坐在筆電鍵盤上、踩在書本上索取注意，原創主子語錄

zh-tw.content:
你在看的那本書、那個螢幕、那份報告——

本喵已經幫你看完了。
結論是：你該看我了。

不客氣。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給總是被貓踩鍵盤、回訊息變成一串亂碼的朋友——他一定遇過 asdkjfh1289 這種狀況
variations: 進階版：我坐在你的鍵盤上，不是擋路，是替你決定今天到此為止。
土味版：我擋住你的螢幕，是因為螢幕裡沒有我——而你看著我的時候，眼神最溫柔。

en.content:
That book, that screen, that report you're reading —

I've already finished it for you.
Conclusion: it's time to look at me.

You're welcome.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend whose texts keep coming out as keyboard mush from a cat walking across it — they've definitely sent an 'asdkjfh1289' before
variations: Advanced version: I sit on your keyboard not to block you, but to decide on your behalf that today is done.
Sweet version: I block your screen because I'm not in it — and your eyes go softest when you're looking at me instead.

items.sourceNote: 經典貓咪語錄『沒有人能擁有一隻貓』概念改寫，原創主子語錄

zh-tw.content:
搞清楚一件事：

房子是你的名字，
帳單是你在繳，
但這個家——

是本喵在管。

你只是付錢的房客。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那種嘴上說『我養了一隻貓』、實際上每天看主子臉色過活的朋友——他會邊笑邊承認自己只是個管家
variations: 進階版：你以為你領養了我？不，是我面試過你，決定讓你留下來伺候。
土味版：這個家我說了算，但我願意把睡你大腿的權利，永久保留給你一個人。

en.content:
Let's get one thing straight:

Your name is on the lease,
you pay the bills,
but this home —

I run it.

You're just the tenant with the credit card.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who claims they 'own a cat' but actually lives entirely at their cat's mercy — they'll laugh and admit they're just the staff
variations: Advanced version: You think you adopted me? No. I interviewed you and decided to let you stay and serve.
Sweet version: I run this house, but the right to be my favorite lap is reserved for you, permanently.

items.sourceNote: 『我沒有忽略你，只是你不是我的優先事項』語錄改寫，原創主子語錄

zh-tw.content:
我不是在忽略你。

我只是把你排在
午睡、曬太陽、舔毛、
再午睡的後面。

別擔心，名單上有你。
排第七而已。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那種對貓掏心掏肺、卻常常被一個眼神打發的朋友——傲嬌主子的精髓就是『我有空再理你』
variations: 進階版：你叫我的時候我沒回頭，不是沒聽到，是我在評估這次值不值得移動。
土味版：我把你排在所有事情的後面，但你是我『睡飽之後第一個想看的臉』。

en.content:
I'm not ignoring you.

I've just placed you
after my nap, my sunbeam,
my grooming, and my second nap.

Don't worry — you made the list.
You're seventh.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who pours their whole heart into their cat and gets dismissed with a single slow blink — peak aloof-cat energy
variations: Advanced version: When you call and I don't turn around, I heard you. I'm just calculating whether you're worth getting up for.
Sweet version: You rank below everything on my list — but you're the first face I want to see after a good nap.

items.sourceNote: 貓咪凌晨三點 FRAP（暴衝）行為觀察，原創主子語錄

zh-tw.content:
凌晨三點。

全家都睡了。
本喵突然想起——
我是一隻野生獵豹。

於是用時速六十公里
衝過你的臉，
撞翻床頭的水杯，
再回去睡。

本能無法解釋。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那種半夜固定被貓踩臉、聽見走廊傳來賽車聲的鏟屎官——這就是傳說中的『午夜暴衝』，每隻貓都會
variations: 進階版：我不是失眠，我是在執行祖先傳下來的夜間巡邏任務，順便確認地板還在。
土味版：我半夜衝過你的臉，不是吵你，是想確認你還在——確認完，我就安心睡了。

en.content:
3 a.m.

The whole house is asleep.
Suddenly I remember —
I am a wild cheetah.

So I sprint across your face
at forty miles an hour,
knock the water off the nightstand,
and go back to sleep.

Instinct cannot be explained.
en.editorNote: Send to the cat owner who gets woken at 3 a.m. by paws on the face and the sound of a tiny race car in the hallway — these are the legendary zoomies, and every cat has them
variations: Advanced version: I'm not an insomniac. I'm running the ancestral night patrol my forebears handed down — and confirming the floor is still there.
Sweet version: I sprint across your face at 3 a.m. not to wake you, but to check you're still here. Once I know, I sleep easy.

items.sourceNote: 『貓奴的職責：餵食、擁抱、被無視』語錄改寫，原創主子語錄

zh-tw.content:
鏟屎官的工作內容：

準時開飯。
隨時待命摸摸。
全心全意被無視。

薪水：零。
福利：偶爾一個賞臉的呼嚕。
離職率：零——
因為你捨不得。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每天心甘情願伺候主子、卻常常被晾在一旁的朋友——『被無視』本身就是這份工作的核心職責
variations: 進階版：這份工作沒有升遷，沒有假期，但有一個會在你最累的時候，悄悄踩上你大腿的老闆。
土味版：我是你零薪水的員工，但你那一聲呼嚕，就是我這輩子收過最好的紅利。

en.content:
Cat servant job description:

Serve meals on time.
Be available for pets at all hours.
Get ignored, wholeheartedly.

Salary: zero.
Benefits: the occasional gracious purr.
Turnover rate: zero —
because you could never quit.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who happily waits on their cat all day and still gets left on read — being ignored is literally in the job description
variations: Advanced version: No promotions, no vacation days — but a boss who quietly climbs into your lap exactly when you're most worn out.
Sweet version: I'm your unpaid employee, but that one purr is the best bonus I'll ever earn.

items.sourceNote: 『貓不是無視你，只是把你排得很後面』語錄改寫，原創主子語錄

zh-tw.content:
主子不是在無視你。

牠只是把你排在
紙箱、陽光、那隻路過的鳥、
還有牠自己的尾巴後面。

你有上榜，已經很給面子了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個老是抱怨「我家貓都不理我」的朋友——能擠進主子的待辦清單，本身就是一種榮幸
variations: 進階版：當主子終於肯轉頭看你一眼，記得珍惜，那是限量發行的眼神。
土味版：在牠心裡你也許排不上第一，但在我心裡你永遠是榜首啦。

en.content:
Your cat isn't ignoring you.

It's just ranked you below
the cardboard box, the sunbeam, that bird outside,
and its own tail.

Making the list at all is an honor.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who complains 'my cat never pays attention to me' — being on the cat's priority list at all is a privilege
variations: Advanced version: When your cat finally turns to look at you, treasure it. That eye contact is a limited edition.
Sweet version: You might not rank first in your cat's heart, but you're always number one in mine.

items.sourceNote: 貓咪半夜暴衝（zoomies）行為觀察改寫，原創主子語錄

zh-tw.content:
凌晨三點。
全家熟睡。

主子突然從房間這頭，
以時速八十衝到另一頭，
撞牆、轉身、再衝回來。

沒有人在追牠。
牠只是想起了，自己曾經是隻獵豹。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個被半夜暴衝吵醒過的貓奴——這不是夢，是主子在練習牠的野性
variations: 附帶說明：暴衝結束後，主子會立刻倒地睡死，彷彿什麼都沒發生過。
土味版：你半夜在我心裡暴衝，撞得我整顆心都是你。

en.content:
3 AM.
The whole house is asleep.

Suddenly the cat rockets from one end of the room
to the other at full speed,
hits the wall, spins around, and charges back.

Nothing is chasing it.
It just remembered it used to be a cheetah.
en.editorNote: Send to the cat owner who's been jolted awake by the 3 AM zoomies — it's not a dream, it's your cat rehearsing the wild
variations: Side note: once the zoomies end, the cat collapses and sleeps like nothing ever happened.
Sweet version: You give my heart the zoomies at 3 AM, bouncing off every wall in here.

items.sourceNote: 『If it fits, I sits』經典貓咪邏輯改寫，原創主子語錄

zh-tw.content:
你花了三千塊買的貓窩：被無視。

網購寄來的那個紙箱：天選寶座。

主子的座右銘很簡單——
塞得下，我就坐。
塞不下，我也硬要坐。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個剛把昂貴貓窩買回家、結果貓只愛紙箱的朋友——這是每個貓奴的共同宿命
variations: 進階版：箱子越小，主子越要把自己折成一坨塞進去，這是物理學無法解釋的奇蹟。
土味版：我這顆心就是你的紙箱，再小你都塞得進來，而且我還巴不得。

en.content:
The $90 cat bed you bought: ignored.

The cardboard box your order came in: a chosen throne.

The cat's motto is simple —
if it fits, I sits.
If it doesn't fit, I sits anyway.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who just splurged on a fancy cat bed only for the cat to live in a box — it's every cat owner's shared fate
variations: Advanced version: the smaller the box, the harder the cat folds itself in. Physics cannot explain this.
Sweet version: My heart is your cardboard box — no matter how small, you'll always fit, and I'd want nothing less.

items.sourceNote: 貓咪佔據筆電鍵盤行為觀察改寫，原創主子語錄

zh-tw.content:
你正在趕一份很重要的報告。

主子緩緩走來，
看了一眼螢幕，
然後一屁股坐上鍵盤正中央。

在牠的邏輯裡：
你盯著看的東西，一定很溫暖、很重要。
所以那個位置，現在是牠的了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個在家工作、總是被貓佔據鍵盤的朋友——在主子眼中，你的專注力就是最高級的暖墊
variations: 附帶觀察：主子坐下後還會回頭看你一眼，那眼神在說：「現在這份報告，由我接手。」
土味版：你就是我最想佔據的那個位置，誰來我都不讓。

en.content:
You're racing to finish an important report.

The cat strolls over,
glances at the screen,
and plants itself dead center on the keyboard.

In its logic:
whatever you stare at must be warm and important.
So that spot belongs to the cat now.
en.editorNote: Send to the work-from-home friend whose keyboard is permanently occupied — to your cat, your focus is the premium heated seat
variations: Side observation: after sitting down, the cat looks back at you as if to say, 'I'll be taking this report from here.'
Sweet version: You're the spot I most want to claim, and I'm not giving it up to anyone.

items.sourceNote: 『主子賞臉看你一眼是國定假日』語錄改寫，原創主子語錄

zh-tw.content:
主子對你的愛，是有營業時間的。

白天：請勿打擾。
傍晚：餵食時段，可酌量呼嚕。
半夜兩點：突然跳上床，踩你的臉，要你陪牠。

而你，居然還覺得感動。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個被貓半夜踩臉、卻一臉幸福的朋友——主子的愛雖然限時供應，但每一次都讓你甘願失眠
variations: 進階版：主子賞你一聲呼嚕，等於頒給你一座年度最佳鏟屎官獎。
土味版：你要是半夜踩我的臉叫我陪你，我大概也會笑著醒過來。

en.content:
Your cat's love comes with business hours.

Daytime: do not disturb.
Evening: feeding window, light purring available.
2 AM: leaps onto the bed, walks across your face, demands company.

And somehow, you find this touching.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who gets a face full of paws at 2 AM and still looks blissful — the cat's love is limited-hours, but every visit is worth the lost sleep
variations: Advanced version: one purr from your cat is basically being handed the Servant of the Year award.
Sweet version: If you walked across my face at 2 AM asking for company, I'd probably wake up smiling too.

items.sourceNote: 『If it fits I sits』經典貓邏輯改寫，原創主子語錄

zh-tw.content:
主子的占領法則：

裝得下，就坐進去。
裝不下，還是硬要坐進去。

紙箱、洗衣籃、剛好比身體小一號的碗——
只要那是一個凹陷，那就是牠的王座。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個花大錢買貓窩、結果貓只睡旁邊那個空紙箱的朋友——你買的不是貓窩，是給自己看的安慰獎
variations: 進階版：你在地上貼一個膠帶圈，主子也會走進去坐好。牠不是笨，牠是尊重規則。
土味版：只要地上有個框框，那就是我的位子，誰都別想跟我搶。

en.content:
The overlord's law of occupation:

If it fits, it sits.
If it doesn't fit, it still sits.

A box, a laundry basket, a bowl one size too small —
if there's a dent in the world, that is now its throne.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who spent good money on a cat bed only to watch the cat sleep in the empty box beside it — that bed was a consolation prize for you
variations: Advanced version: tape a square on the floor and your cat will walk in and sit. It's not dumb. It simply respects the rules.
Plain version: If there's a shape on the floor, that's my spot now, and nobody's taking it.

items.sourceNote: 貓咪無視昂貴玩具偏愛空紙箱行為，原創主子語錄

zh-tw.content:
你花了八百塊買一個會自己動的逗貓玩具。

主子看了一眼，
轉身鑽進那個玩具寄來的紙箱裡。

從此玩具放在角落生灰，
紙箱被牠睡到變形。

這就是品味。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個家裡堆滿沒被寵幸過的貓玩具的朋友——主子要的從來不是玩具，是那個免費附贈的盒子
variations: 進階版：你買的東西越貴，主子越愛它的包裝。下次直接寄空箱給牠就好。
土味版：玩具我看都不看，紙箱我能睡一整個夏天。

en.content:
You spent twenty-five bucks on a self-moving cat toy.

The overlord glanced at it once,
then climbed into the box it shipped in.

The toy now collects dust in a corner.
The box has been slept into a new shape.

This is what taste looks like.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend whose home is a graveyard of unloved cat toys — the cat never wanted the toy, just the free box it came in
variations: Advanced version: the more you spend, the more your cat loves the packaging. Next time, just mail it the empty box.
Plain version: Won't even look at the toy. Can sleep in the cardboard box all summer.

items.sourceNote: 貓咪半夜暴衝 zoomies 行為，原創主子語錄

zh-tw.content:
凌晨三點，全家都睡了。

主子忽然像被按下開關，
從客廳一路衝刺到房間，
撞翻一個東西，再衝回去，
然後若無其事坐下舔手手。

沒人知道剛剛發生什麼，
包括牠自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個被半夜暴衝主子吵醒、躺在黑暗中懷疑人生的朋友——這不是惡夢，這是養貓的日常
variations: 進階版：科學家還沒搞懂貓的半夜暴衝，主子也不打算解釋。
土味版：我自己也不知道我剛剛在衝三小，但衝就對了。

en.content:
3 AM. The whole house is asleep.

The overlord flips an invisible switch,
sprints full speed from the living room to the bedroom,
knocks something over, sprints back,
then sits down and casually licks a paw.

Nobody knows what just happened.
The cat included.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend jolted awake by midnight zoomies, lying in the dark questioning their life — this isn't a nightmare, it's just cat ownership
variations: Advanced version: scientists still can't fully explain the 3 AM zoomies, and the overlord has no plans to comment.
Plain version: Honestly I don't know what I was sprinting for either, but I committed to it.

items.sourceNote: 『nobody owns a cat / 貓付帳單由你』語錄改寫，原創主子語錄

zh-tw.content:
每個養貓的人遲早都會明白一件事：

沒有人能擁有一隻貓。

你只是那個負責付帳單、鏟貓砂、
半夜被踩醒、
還要對牠的存在心存感激的人。

這個家是主子的，
你只是合法房客。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個自稱是「貓主人」的朋友，溫柔地提醒他搞錯身分了——你不是主人，你是被選中的鏟屎官
variations: 進階版：你以為你養了一隻貓，其實是一隻貓決定讓你留下來。
土味版：這個家我說了算，你負責付房租就好。

en.content:
Every cat person eventually learns one truth:

Nobody owns a cat.

You are simply the one who pays the bills, scoops the litter,
gets walked on at 2 AM,
and is expected to feel grateful for the privilege.

This house belongs to the overlord.
You are merely the lawful tenant.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who calls themselves a 'cat owner' — gently inform them they've got the title wrong; they were chosen, not in charge
variations: Advanced version: you think you got a cat. Actually, a cat decided to let you stay.
Plain version: I run this house. You just handle the rent.

items.sourceNote: 『cats don't ignore you, they rank you low / 排名低』語錄改寫，原創主子語錄

zh-tw.content:
你以為主子在無視你。

其實牠沒有無視你，
牠只是把你排在很後面。

罐罐排第一，
陽光排第二，
那個紙箱排第三，

你大概在……
窗外那隻鳥的後面一點。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個抱怨「我家貓都不理我」的朋友，殘忍但溫柔地告訴他真相——不是不理，是排名問題
variations: 進階版：別難過，至少你還在榜上，吸塵器連入圍都沒有。
土味版：我有在乎你啊，只是順位剛好在罐罐後面而已。

en.content:
You think your cat is ignoring you.

It is not ignoring you.
It has simply ranked you very low.

The can of food is number one.
The sunny spot is number two.
That cardboard box is number three.

You are somewhere just behind
the bird outside the window.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who complains 'my cat never pays attention to me' — break the news gently; it's not neglect, it's a ranking issue
variations: Advanced version: cheer up, at least you made the list. The vacuum cleaner didn't even get nominated.
Plain version: I do care about you. You just rank slightly below the food can.

items.sourceNote: 『acts starving but leaves half the food / 裝餓但剩一半』語錄改寫，原創主子語錄

zh-tw.content:
主子在你面前哀號，
彷彿已經三天沒進食，
眼神空洞，命在旦夕。

你心疼地倒了一碗。

牠吃了三口，
抬頭看你一眼，
優雅地走開，
留下大半碗。

那不是餓，
那是行為藝術。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個天天被主子用「快餓死」眼神勒索、結果飼料還是剩一半的朋友——你被演了
variations: 進階版：碗裡還有，但牠堅持那是「舊的」，要你倒一碗一模一樣的新的。
土味版：我不是餓，我只是想確認你還願意為我倒飯。

en.content:
The overlord wails at your feet,
as if it hasn't eaten in three days,
eyes hollow, clinging to life.

Moved, you pour a fresh bowl.

It takes three bites,
looks up at you,
strolls away with great dignity,
and leaves more than half behind.

That wasn't hunger.
That was performance art.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend blackmailed daily by a 'starving' cat that still leaves half the bowl — you've been played
variations: Advanced version: there's food in the bowl, but it insists that batch is 'old' and demands an identical fresh one.
Plain version: I'm not hungry. I just needed to confirm you'd still pour for me.

items.sourceNote: 『walk like they own the place, look mildly disappointed / 走路像房東、看你像失望』語錄改寫，原創主子語錄

zh-tw.content:
主子走路的樣子，
像是這整棟房子都是牠的，
而你只是個遲交租金的房客。

牠看你的眼神，
帶著一點點失望，
卻又在你坐下的瞬間，
理所當然地跳上你的腿。

嫌棄你，
但又離不開你。
這就是主子的愛。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個被主子嫌棄一整天、晚上卻被踩在腿上呼嚕的朋友——這就是傲嬌的最高境界
variations: 進階版：牠用全身嫌棄你，再用全身重量壓在你身上，邏輯一致。
土味版：我看你不順眼，但你的腿我要定了。

en.content:
The overlord walks
as if it owns the entire house
and you're just a tenant behind on rent.

It looks at you
with a faint air of disappointment,
yet the moment you sit down,
it claims your lap like it's a birthright.

Unimpressed by you,
yet unable to leave you.
This is how a cat loves.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend judged all day by their cat, only to get purred at from their lap by night — this is tsundere in its purest form
variations: Advanced version: it judges you with its whole body, then pins you down with its full weight. Perfectly consistent.
Plain version: I'm not impressed by you, but that lap is mine now.

items.sourceNote: 『cats triggered by the word dinner / 一聽到開飯就現身』行為改寫，原創主子語錄

zh-tw.content:
主子可以一整天不見蹤影，
你翻箱倒櫃，喊到聲音沙啞，
牠依然如人間蒸發。

但只要你輕輕拉開罐罐的拉環——

喀。

牠就會以違反物理的速度，
從某個你不知道的次元
瞬間出現在你腳邊。

牠不是消失，
牠只是在等那一聲。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個怎麼找都找不到貓、結果開個罐頭就立刻被包圍的朋友——主子的聽力是有選擇性的
variations: 進階版：你叫牠名字一百次牠沒反應，零食袋摩擦聲一次就到位。
土味版：我不是不理你，是你剛剛沒有發出開飯的聲音。

en.content:
The overlord can vanish for an entire day.
You search every closet, call until you're hoarse,
and it stays gone like it evaporated.

But the instant you peel back
the lid of a food can —

click.

It materializes at your feet
at physics-defying speed,
teleporting in from some dimension
you didn't know existed.

It never disappeared.
It was just waiting for that sound.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who can never find their cat until a can opens and they're suddenly surrounded — feline hearing is highly selective
variations: Advanced version: call its name a hundred times, nothing. Crinkle the treat bag once, instant arrival.
Plain version: I wasn't ignoring you. You just hadn't made the dinner sound yet.

items.sourceNote: 『if it fits I sits / 貓鑽進過小空間』迷因改寫，原創主子語錄

zh-tw.content:
你花了三千塊
買了一張超舒服的貓窩。

主子看了一眼，
轉身鑽進那個裝貓窩的
空紙箱裡。

貓的原則只有一條：
裝得下，我就坐。
裝不下，我硬要坐。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個剛幫貓買了高級窩、結果貓只愛紙箱的朋友——他現在一定在懷疑人生
variations: 進階版：箱子越小，主子越想證明自己塞得進去，而且還要在裡面睡得理直氣壯。
土味版：我不是嫌你的禮物，我只是更愛免費的那個。

en.content:
You spent a hundred bucks
on a deluxe, ultra-plush cat bed.

The overlord glances at it,
turns around, and climbs into
the empty cardboard box
the bed came in.

A cat has exactly one rule:
if it fits, I sits.
If it doesn't fit, I sits anyway.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who just bought a premium cat bed only to watch their cat live in the box it shipped in
variations: Advanced version: the smaller the box, the more determined the cat is to prove it fits — and then nap in it with full confidence.
Plain version: I'm not rejecting your gift. I just like the free one more.

items.sourceNote: 『3 AM zoomies / 半夜衝刺 frapping』行為改寫，原創主子語錄

zh-tw.content:
主子一整天睡了十八個小時。

凌晨三點，
你睡得正熟，
突然一陣狂奔的腳步聲
從走廊另一端衝來，
撞翻一個杯子，
再用尾巴掃過你的臉。

這不是失控。
這是牠精心安排的
夜間運動會。
而你，是唯一的觀眾。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個常常被貓半夜衝刺吵醒的朋友——主子的體力是有排程的，而且只在你最想睡的時候啟動
variations: 進階版：白天叫牠玩牠懶得理你，凌晨三點牠自己開趴，全程不需要你同意。
土味版：我白天養精蓄銳，就是為了晚上陪你一起清醒。

en.content:
The overlord slept eighteen hours today.

At 3 a.m.,
just as you drift into deep sleep,
a thunder of paws
comes tearing down the hallway,
knocks over a cup,
and sweeps a tail across your face.

This is not chaos.
This is a carefully scheduled
nighttime track meet.
And you are the only spectator.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who gets woken at 3 a.m. by feline sprints — cat energy runs on a strict schedule, activated precisely when you most want sleep
variations: Advanced version: invite the cat to play during the day and it ignores you. At 3 a.m. it throws its own party, no permission required.
Plain version: I conserved energy all day just so I could keep you company while awake tonight.

items.sourceNote: 『cats bring dead gifts / 貓叼獵物當禮物』行為改寫，原創主子語錄

zh-tw.content:
主子把一隻
（已經沒有生命跡象的）小東西
放在你枕頭旁。

你尖叫。

但牠的表情很平靜，
因為在牠的世界裡，
這是最高等級的禮物，
是牠教你怎麼狩獵的
第一堂課。

牠不是嚇你，
牠是把你當家人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個被貓送過「驚喜禮物」、嚇到但又有點感動的朋友——這其實是主子在用牠的方式說愛你
variations: 進階版：牠覺得你不會打獵，活不下去，所以決定養你。
土味版：我抓的，分你一半，這就是我們家的規矩。

en.content:
The overlord places a small
(no longer living) creature
right beside your pillow.

You scream.

But its expression stays calm,
because in its world
this is the highest tier of gift —
the very first lesson
in how to hunt.

It isn't trying to scare you.
It's treating you as family.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who got a 'surprise gift' from their cat and was horrified but secretly touched — this is how the overlord says it loves you
variations: Advanced version: it has decided you can't possibly hunt for yourself, so it's chosen to provide for you.
Plain version: I caught it, and I'm sharing half with you. That's how it works in this house.

items.sourceNote: 『cats don't ignore you, they rank you low / 貓不是無視你，是把你排名很後面』語錄改寫，原創主子語錄

zh-tw.content:
主子不是在無視你。

牠只是
在心裡的優先順序清單上，
把你排在
那束照進來的陽光、
那個會動的塑膠袋、
還有牆上那隻看不見的蟲
後面而已。

別難過，
你至少有上榜。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個常常覺得自己被貓冷落的朋友——你不是不重要，你只是排在陽光後面而已，這很正常
variations: 進階版：當你不在的時候你的排名會瞬間上升，因為沒人開罐罐。
土味版：我不是不愛你，是這束陽光剛好比你早一步出現。

en.content:
The overlord isn't ignoring you.

It has simply ranked you,
on its internal priority list,
somewhere below
that patch of sunlight,
the plastic bag that moves on its own,
and the invisible bug on the wall.

Don't feel bad.
At least you made the list.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who feels snubbed by their cat — you're not unimportant, you're just ranked below the sunbeam, and that's completely normal
variations: Advanced version: your ranking spikes the moment you leave, because no one else opens the cans.
Plain version: I don't love you any less. The sunbeam just got here first.

items.sourceNote: 『cats don't have owners, they have staff / 貓沒有主人只有員工』語錄改寫，原創主子語錄

zh-tw.content:
別再說你「養」了一隻貓。

你沒有養牠，
是你通過了面試，
被錄取成牠的全職員工：

負責開罐、鏟屎、供暖、
以及在牠想被摸的時候
精準出現，
不想被摸的時候
立刻消失。

沒有薪水，
而且隨時可能被牠用眼神資遣。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每一個自稱「貓奴」的朋友——這份工作沒有薪水、沒有假期，但他們甘之如飴
variations: 進階版：年度考核由主子主持，標準浮動，唯一確定的是你永遠不會升職。
土味版：我不是你的主人，我是你心甘情願加班的那個理由。

en.content:
Stop saying you 'own' a cat.

You don't own it.
You passed the interview
and were hired as its full-time staff:

in charge of opening cans, scooping litter,
providing warmth, appearing precisely
when it wants to be petted,
and vanishing instantly
when it does not.

No salary.
And you can be let go
with a single look, at any time.
en.editorNote: Send to every friend who calls themselves a 'cat servant' — no pay, no vacation, and they wouldn't trade it for anything
variations: Advanced version: your annual review is conducted by the cat, the criteria float, and the one certainty is you'll never be promoted.
Plain version: I'm not your owner. I'm the reason you happily work overtime.

items.sourceNote: 貓咪無視昂貴玩具只愛紙箱，原創主子語錄

zh-tw.content:
你花三百塊買的逗貓棒：
聞一下，走開。

你花八百塊買的貓跳台：
看一眼，走開。

那個棒子寄來時裝的紙箱：
這是朕的宮殿，誰都不准動。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個花大錢買玩具、結果貓只玩紙箱的朋友——他家現在一定還留著一個捨不得丟的破箱子
variations: 進階版：玩具越貴，被無視的速度越快；這是一條穩定到可以寫進物理課本的定律。
土味版：我要的從來不是玩具，是那個免費的紙箱，謝謝。

en.content:
The $30 feather wand you bought:
Sniff once. Walk away.

The $80 cat tower you assembled:
One glance. Walk away.

The cardboard box the wand shipped in:
This is my palace. No one may touch it.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who spent real money on cat toys and still has a beat-up box they're not allowed to throw out
variations: Advanced version: the more expensive the toy, the faster it gets ignored — a law stable enough to print in a physics textbook.
Plain version: I never wanted the toy. I wanted the free box. Thanks.

items.sourceNote: 貓咪半夜暴衝 FRAP 行為，原創主子語錄

zh-tw.content:
凌晨三點十七分。

朕忽然想起一件
非常緊急的事：
要從客廳全速衝到房間，
撞翻一個垃圾桶，
再衝回來。

什麼事？
你不會懂，這是機密。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每個被貓半夜暴衝吵醒、躺在床上問人生意義的養貓人——他剛剛一定又被踩過肚子
variations: 進階版：暴衝路線經過精密計算，務必在你剛睡著的那一秒鐘啟動，效果最好。
土味版：我不是失眠，我是在巡視領土，順便提醒你誰才是這裡的主人。

en.content:
3:17 a.m.

I have suddenly remembered
something extremely urgent:
sprint from the living room to the bedroom
at full speed, knock over one trash can,
then sprint back.

What is it?
You wouldn't understand. It's classified.
en.editorNote: Send to every cat owner woken at 3 a.m. by the zoomies — they're lying there questioning their life choices right now
variations: Advanced version: the route is precisely calculated to launch the exact second you finally fall asleep, for maximum effect.
Plain version: I'm not an insomniac. I'm patrolling my territory and reminding you who's in charge.

items.sourceNote: 貓咪坐在電腦鍵盤上行為，原創主子語錄

zh-tw.content:
你在打字，
表示你在注意螢幕，
而不是注意朕。

這個問題很好解決：
朕直接坐在鍵盤上。

現在你只能看朕了。
不客氣。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個一開電腦、貓就準時坐上鍵盤的居家工作朋友——他的會議一定被「fjjjjjjjj」洗版過
variations: 進階版：朕會精準挑在你打到一半、句子最關鍵的那個字時坐上去，這叫掌握節奏。
土味版：你工作賺的錢是要養我的，所以監工也是一種貢獻。

en.content:
You're typing,
which means you're looking at the screen
and not at me.

This is easily solved:
I will sit directly on the keyboard.

Now you can only look at me.
You're welcome.
en.editorNote: Send to the work-from-home friend whose cat lands on the keyboard the moment the laptop opens — their meetings have been flooded with 'fjjjjjjjj'
variations: Advanced version: I time the landing for the exact key in the most important word of your sentence. That's called rhythm.
Plain version: the money you earn working feeds me, so supervising you is a contribution too.

items.sourceNote: 貓咪情緒勒索捨不得丟的垃圾，原創主子語錄

zh-tw.content:
那個你想丟掉的破紙袋，
那個皺巴巴的瓶蓋，
那條卡在沙發底下的橡皮筋——

那不是垃圾。
那是朕的情緒支持物。

你動它一根毛，
今晚就睡客廳。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給家裡有一堆「不能丟」的貓咪私人收藏的朋友——他清過一次桌子就被主子盯了三天
variations: 進階版：那條橡皮筋朕已經追了兩個月，它有名字，有故事，你沒資格決定它的去留。
土味版：在我這裡，垃圾跟寶物的差別只有一個——我喜不喜歡。

en.content:
That ratty paper bag you want to toss,
that crumpled bottle cap,
that hair tie stuck under the couch —

That is not trash.
That is my emotional support item.

Touch a single hair of it
and you're sleeping on the couch tonight.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend whose home is full of cat-approved junk they're forbidden to throw out — they cleared a table once and got side-eyed for three days
variations: Advanced version: I've been chasing that hair tie for two months. It has a name and a backstory, and you don't get a vote on its fate.
Plain version: around here, the only difference between trash and treasure is whether I like it.

items.sourceNote: 貓咪天使惡魔瞬間切換行為，原創主子語錄

zh-tw.content:
前一秒，
朕窩在你胸口，
眼神溫柔，呼嚕作響，
像一團會發熱的棉花。

下一秒，
朕從書架頂端，
把你三個馬克杯一一推下。

是的，這兩個都是朕。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個被貓一秒切換天使惡魔模式、永遠抓不準的養貓人——他現在一定一邊心軟一邊掃碎片
variations: 進階版：天使模式只是充電，惡魔模式才是出廠設定，別搞錯了。
土味版：我可愛是給你看的，我搗蛋是給你記住的，兩個都要愛。

en.content:
One second,
I'm curled on your chest,
eyes soft, purring,
like a warm ball of cotton.

The next second,
from the top of the bookshelf,
I knock all three of your mugs off, one by one.

Yes. Both of these are me.
en.editorNote: Send to the cat owner who can never predict the angel-to-demon switch — they're melting and sweeping up shards at the same time right now
variations: Advanced version: angel mode is just recharging. Demon mode is the factory default. Don't get it twisted.
Plain version: the cute is for you to enjoy, the chaos is for you to remember. Love both.

items.sourceNote: 貓咪凌晨三點暴衝（zoomies）行為觀察，原創改寫主子語錄

zh-tw.content:
凌晨三點，
全世界都睡了。

而朕，
剛剛完成熱身。

從走廊衝刺到沙發，
從沙發彈到衣櫃頂，
再以一聲驚天慘叫宣告：
夜間訓練，正式開始。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個總是被半夜暴衝聲嚇醒、卻又捨不得生氣的養貓人——他昨晚一定也被吵醒過
variations: 進階版：朕不是失眠，朕是在維持戰鬥狀態，畢竟誰知道天亮前會不會有一隻看不見的敵人入侵。
土味版：你睡你的，我跑我的，我們各自精彩，互不打擾——除了我會踩過你的臉。

en.content:
3 AM.
The whole world is asleep.

And I
have just finished warming up.

Sprint down the hallway,
launch off the couch,
scale the wardrobe,
then announce with one bloodcurdling scream:
night training has officially begun.
en.editorNote: Send to the cat owner jolted awake by midnight zoomies who somehow still can't be mad — they got woken up last night too
variations: Advanced version: I'm not an insomniac. I'm staying combat-ready. Who knows when an invisible enemy might invade before dawn.
Plain version: you do your sleeping, I'll do my sprinting. We each shine in our own way — except I'll be running across your face.

items.sourceNote: 「if it fits I sits」貓咪鑽箱子經典迷因，原創改寫主子語錄

zh-tw.content:
規則很簡單：
裝得下，朕就坐。

紙箱、洗衣籃、你的後背包、
那個比朕小一半的玻璃碗——

裝不下？
那是你的問題，不是朕的。
朕會硬塞進去。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個花大錢買貓窩、結果貓只愛睡網購紙箱的朋友——他家現在一定有一堆捨不得丟的空箱
variations: 進階版：你買的那個貓窩很貴對吧？朕知道。所以朕選擇睡在它旁邊的箱子裡，純粹是為了讓你清醒一點。
土味版：能塞就塞，塞不下就擠到塞下為止，這就是朕的人生哲學。

en.content:
The rule is simple:
if it fits, I sits.

Cardboard box, laundry basket, your backpack,
that glass bowl half my size —

Doesn't fit?
That's your problem, not mine.
I'll force it.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who spent a fortune on a cat bed only for the cat to sleep in the delivery box — their place is full of empty boxes they can't throw out
variations: Advanced version: that cat bed you bought was expensive, wasn't it? I know. That's exactly why I sleep in the box next to it. Just to keep you humble.
Plain version: if it fits, I sit; if it doesn't, I squeeze until it does. That's my whole philosophy.

items.sourceNote: 貓咪對著空無一物的牆角或天花板凝視/嚎叫行為，原創改寫主子語錄

zh-tw.content:
你看到的是：
朕對著空蕩蕩的牆角，
突然瞳孔放大，
發出一聲低沉的嚎叫。

朕看到的是：
你這輩子都不該知道的東西。

相信朕，
還是不要問比較好。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個被貓半夜盯著牆角看到頭皮發麻的朋友——他絕對回頭確認過那裡有沒有東西
variations: 進階版：朕不是在嚇你，朕是在保護你。那個東西，朕已經盯了一整晚，你安心睡吧。
土味版：你問我看什麼？看了你也看不到，看到了你也睡不著，所以還是別問了。

en.content:
What you see:
I stare at an empty corner,
pupils suddenly blown wide,
