and let out one low, guttural howl.

What I see:
something you were never meant to know about.

Trust me —
you're better off not asking.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend whose cat stares at empty corners at night until their skin crawls — they definitely turned around to check if anything was there
variations: Advanced version: I'm not trying to scare you. I'm protecting you. I've been watching that thing all night. Sleep easy.
Plain version: you ask what I'm looking at? You wouldn't see it anyway, and if you did you'd never sleep. So don't ask.

items.sourceNote: 貓咪定時叫早討飯（清晨鬧鐘行為）觀察，原創改寫主子語錄

zh-tw.content:
你的鬧鐘設五點半。
朕的鬧鐘設五點整。

朕的鬧鐘比較準，
而且不能按掉。

方法包括但不限於：
踩你的膀胱、
咬你的頭髮、
以及把整盆水推下桌。

起床。朕餓了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個被貓當鬧鐘、永遠睡不到自然醒的養貓人——他每天清晨都在跟一隻毛茸茸的鬧鐘搏鬥
variations: 進階版：朕的碗裡其實還有飼料，但那是昨天的飼料，朕拒絕吃有歷史的食物。
土味版：天還沒亮我就知道——你該起來愛我，順便餵我了。

en.content:
Your alarm is set for 5:30.
Mine is set for 5:00 sharp.

Mine is more accurate,
and you cannot hit snooze.

Methods include but are not limited to:
standing on your bladder,
chewing your hair,
and knocking a full bowl of water off the table.

Get up. I'm hungry.
en.editorNote: Send to the cat owner used as a furry alarm clock who hasn't slept in since adoption — they wrestle a purring alarm every dawn
variations: Advanced version: there is technically still food in my bowl, but it's yesterday's food, and I refuse to eat anything with a history.
Plain version: the sun isn't even up and I already know — it's time for you to love me. And feed me. In that order.

items.sourceNote: 貓咪愛踩鍵盤/坐在主人正在用的東西上面行為，原創改寫主子語錄

zh-tw.content:
整張桌子那麼大，
朕偏偏要坐在你的鍵盤上。

整張床那麼大，
朕偏偏要躺在你正在看的書上。

不是朕故意擋你，
是朕要坐在「你的注意力」上。

那才是朕真正想佔據的位置。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個一打開筆電貓就來踩鍵盤、根本沒辦法好好工作的人——他現在螢幕上一定有一串貓打的亂碼
variations: 進階版：你以為朕在搗亂，其實朕在提醒你——你已經盯著那個發光的盒子太久了，該看朕了。
土味版：你的工作可以等，你的書可以等，但朕想被你看見，不能等。

en.content:
The desk is huge,
yet I choose to sit on your keyboard.

The bed is huge,
yet I choose to lie on the exact book you're reading.

I'm not blocking you on purpose.
I'm sitting on your attention.

That's the spot I actually want to occupy.
en.editorNote: Send to the person whose cat walks across the keyboard the moment the laptop opens — there's a string of cat-typed gibberish on their screen right now
variations: Advanced version: you think I'm being a nuisance. Actually I'm reminding you — you've been staring at that glowing box too long. Look at me instead.
Plain version: your work can wait, your book can wait, but my need to be seen by you cannot wait.

items.sourceNote: 貓咪堅持陪主人上廁所、對關起來的門抓門喵叫的行為觀察，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
全家的門你都能自由進出。
只有一扇門，你進不去。
那扇門後面，我在上廁所。

於是那扇門，成為你此生最大的執念——
門縫底下伸進一隻手，喵到破音。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個連上廁所都有貓在門外抗議的朋友——他此刻大概正一邊蹲著，一邊看門縫底下那隻毛茸茸的爪子揮來揮去
variations: 進階版：門一關，牠立刻覺得你被綁架了，用盡全力搶救，救援方式是抓門加哀嚎。
簡短版：對貓來說，全世界最神祕的房間就是那間你不讓牠進去的廁所。

en.content:
Every door in this house, you can come and go as you please.
Except one.
Behind that one door, I'm in the bathroom.

So that door becomes the great obsession of your life —
a paw jammed under the gap, meowing until your voice cracks.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who can't even pee without a furry protest outside the door — right now they're probably watching a paw wave frantically under the gap
variations: Advanced version: the second that door closes, the cat is convinced you've been kidnapped, and launches a full rescue operation consisting of clawing and wailing.
Short version: to a cat, the most mysterious room on earth is the one bathroom you won't let it into.

items.sourceNote: 貓咪拒喝自己水碗、偏要喝主人杯子裡的水的行為觀察，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
你買了貓咪專用飲水機，會過濾、會循環、還會發光。
我看都不看一眼。

因為我要喝的，是你桌上那杯放了三小時的白開水。
它更好喝。
因為它是你的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個為貓砸大錢買飲水機、結果貓只肯喝馬克杯裡水的朋友——他的杯子這輩子沒有真正屬於過自己
variations: 進階版：你的水杯放哪我都找得到。我自己的水碗？從沒聽說過這種東西。
簡短版：貓咪飲水機是買給你安心的，不是買給我喝的。

en.content:
You bought me a special cat fountain. It filters, it circulates, it even glows.
I don't spare it a glance.

Because what I want to drink is that glass of plain water
that's been sitting on your desk for three hours.
It tastes better.
Because it's yours.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who spent a fortune on a cat fountain only to watch the cat drink exclusively from their mug — that glass was never truly theirs to begin with
variations: Advanced version: I can locate your water glass anywhere in the house. My own water bowl? Never heard of such a thing.
Short version: the cat fountain was bought for your peace of mind, not for me to actually drink from.

items.sourceNote: 貓咪踏踏（making biscuits）行為觀察，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
我趴在你肚子上，兩隻前腳一下一下地踩。
你以為我在按摩你。

不，我是在確認你夠不夠軟。
測試通過。
你被正式認證為合格的床墊。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個被貓當成人肉麵團天天踏踏、還踏到流口水的朋友——他早就分不清這是寵愛還是品管抽檢了
variations: 進階版：踏踏的時候我很放鬆很幸福，代價是你的大腿要承受十根小針。愛是有痛感的。
簡短版：你不是我的主人，你是我從小揉到大的那塊麵團。

en.content:
I lie on your belly and press down with my front paws, one after the other.
You think I'm giving you a massage.

No. I'm checking whether you're soft enough.
Test passed.
You are hereby certified as an acceptable mattress.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who gets kneaded like human dough every day, sometimes with a little drool — they long ago stopped knowing if it's affection or a quality-control inspection
variations: Advanced version: I'm blissed out and relaxed while I knead. The price is ten tiny needles in your thigh. Love comes with a sting.
Short version: you're not my owner. You're the dough I've been kneading since I was a kitten.

items.sourceNote: 貓咪一看到外出籠就消失、去獸醫院前的心理攻防戰，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
外出籠從櫃子裡拿出來的那一秒——
我就消失了。

我沒有跑。
我只是瞬間學會了隱身術，
並縮進了這個家你從來不知道存在的第七維度縫隙裡。
找不到我，就不用去獸醫院。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個每次帶貓看醫生都要先玩半小時捉迷藏的朋友——他大概已經練成趴在地上用手電筒往沙發底下照的專業姿勢了
variations: 進階版：平常八公斤重、動都不想動。一看到外出籠，立刻變成三公斤重、身手矯健的忍者。
簡短版：外出籠一出現，我就啟動全屋躲貓貓模式，難度：地獄級。

en.content:
The second the carrier comes out of the closet —
I vanish.

I didn't run.
I simply achieved instant invisibility,
and folded myself into the seventh-dimensional crevice of this house you never knew existed.
If you can't find me, we can't go to the vet.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who has to play thirty minutes of hide-and-seek before every vet trip — they've probably perfected the flat-on-the-floor, flashlight-under-the-couch pose by now
variations: Advanced version: normally I weigh eight kilos and refuse to move. The moment I see the carrier, I become a three-kilo ninja with reflexes to match.
Short version: carrier appears, whole-house hide-and-seek activates. Difficulty: nightmare mode.

items.sourceNote: 貓咪把四肢收起來變成「土司/麵包」坐姿（loaf mode）的行為觀察，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
我把四隻腳全部收進身體底下。
尾巴繞好，下巴微收。

現在我不是貓了。
我是一顆溫熱、會呼吸、隨時可能翻臉的土司。
請勿觸摸，除非你想被咬。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個看到貓變成土司就忍不住想戳、然後被咬的朋友——他手上大概還留著上次「戳土司」的教訓
variations: 進階版：土司模式代表我很放鬆，但這不表示我允許你摸我肚子。摸了就是你自找的。
簡短版：把腳收起來的那一刻，我就從貓咪升級成一塊沒人敢動的限量土司。

en.content:
I tuck all four legs beneath my body.
Tail wrapped, chin lowered.

I am no longer a cat.
I am a warm, breathing loaf of bread that may turn on you at any moment.
Do not touch. Unless you'd like to be bitten.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who can't resist poking the cat the second it goes into loaf mode, then gets bitten — they probably still have last week's 'poke the loaf' lesson on their hand
variations: Advanced version: loaf mode means I'm relaxed. It does NOT mean you may touch my belly. If you do, that's on you.
Short version: the moment I tuck my legs in, I upgrade from cat to a limited-edition loaf nobody dares to touch.

items.sourceNote: 貓咪凌晨三點暴衝（zoomies）行為觀察，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
白天：睡二十小時，連飼料都懶得走過去吃。

凌晨三點零七分：
突然啟動渦輪引擎，從客廳衝到房間，撞倒垃圾桶，踩過你的臉，再折返一次。

這叫日常運動。
時間我排好了，你不用管。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個每天半夜都被貓踩臉、然後聽到走廊傳來賽車聲的朋友——他大概已經放棄關房門了，因為主子有牠自己的作息表
variations: 進階版：暴衝路線我規劃過了，一定會經過你的頭。這不是巧合，這是安排。
簡短版：你的睡眠不是我的問題。凌晨三點，準時開跑。

en.content:
Daytime: sleep twenty hours, too lazy to even walk to the food bowl.

3:07 a.m.:
turbo engine engaged, sprint from living room to bedroom, knock over the trash can, run across your face, then do it all again in reverse.

This is called daily exercise.
I've scheduled it. You don't need to be involved.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who gets a paw to the face every night and then hears race-car noises down the hallway — they've probably given up closing the bedroom door, because the overlord keeps its own schedule
variations: Advanced version: I've mapped the zoomies route, and it definitely goes across your head. Not a coincidence. A plan.
Short version: your sleep is not my problem. 3 a.m., right on time, the race begins.

items.sourceNote: 貓咪坐在鍵盤上、擋住螢幕行為觀察，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
你在工作。

我不管。

這台會發熱的長方形，現在是我的床。
這塊有很多小按鈕的地方，現在是我的枕頭。
那個一直看的發光板子，現在被我的屁股完全遮住了。

你的會議可以等。
我的午睡不行。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個開視訊會議開到一半、鏡頭裡突然只剩一顆貓屁股的居家工作朋友——他大概已經練成一手打字、一手把貓往旁邊挪的絕技了
variations: 進階版：我坐在鍵盤上不是想睡覺（好啦有一半是），主要是要蓋上我的味道，提醒你——這台電腦，還有你，都是我的。
簡短版：螢幕是給你看的，鍵盤是給我睡的。分工很清楚。

en.content:
You're working.

I don't care.

This warm rectangle is now my bed.
This area with all the little buttons is now my pillow.
That glowing panel you keep staring at is now fully blocked by my butt.

Your meeting can wait.
My nap cannot.
en.editorNote: Send to the work-from-home friend whose video call suddenly became just a cat butt on camera — they've probably mastered typing with one hand while relocating the cat with the other
variations: Advanced version: I sit on the keyboard not just to sleep (okay, half to sleep), but mainly to lay down my scent — a reminder that this laptop, and you, belong to me.
Short version: the screen is for you to look at. The keyboard is for me to sleep on. Clear division of labor.

items.sourceNote: 貓咪盯著牆壁的空白處看行為觀察，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
半夜，我突然坐直。
盯著牆角那個什麼都沒有的地方。
一動也不動，看了整整五分鐘。

你嚇到起雞皮疙瘩，問我：「那裡是不是有什麼？」

有啊。
只是我不打算告訴你。
你安心睡，反正也逃不掉。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個半夜看到貓對著牆壁發呆、然後整晚不敢關燈的朋友——他這輩子都不會知道牆角到底有什麼，這就是主子最愛的效果
variations: 進階版：我不是在看牆，我是在看一隻你看不見的蟲。或者是別的東西。這個問題，你最好別深究。
簡短版：牆角有東西。我看得到，你看不到。晚安。

en.content:
In the middle of the night, I suddenly sit up straight.
Staring at that corner of the wall where there's absolutely nothing.
Motionless, for a full five minutes.

You get goosebumps and ask me, 'Is there something over there?'

Yes.
I just don't plan on telling you.
Sleep tight. It's not like you can escape anyway.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who caught their cat staring at a blank wall at 2 a.m. and then couldn't turn off the lights all night — they'll never know what's in that corner, which is exactly how the overlord likes it
variations: Advanced version: I'm not staring at the wall. I'm watching a bug you can't see. Or something else. This is a question you'd rather not dig into.
Short version: there's something in that corner. I can see it, you can't. Goodnight.

items.sourceNote: 貓咪被摸肚子後突然咬人／貓咪陷阱（belly trap）行為觀察，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
我翻過身，露出圓滾滾、毛茸茸、看起來超級想被摸的肚子。

你伸出手。

這就是陷阱。
這一直都是陷阱。
我的肚子不是邀請，是測驗——
而你，剛剛沒通過。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個每次看到貓露肚子就忍不住想摸、然後手上多幾道抓痕的朋友——他到現在還是學不乖，因為圓肚子的誘惑實在太大了
variations: 進階版：翻肚子代表我信任你。摸下去代表你辜負了這份信任。這兩件事不衝突。
簡短版：露肚子是給你看的，不是給你摸的。分清楚，手才保得住。

en.content:
I roll over, exposing a round, fluffy belly that looks like it desperately wants to be petted.

You reach out your hand.

This is a trap.
It was always a trap.
My belly is not an invitation, it's a test —
and you just failed it.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who can never resist a fluffy exposed belly and always walks away with fresh scratches — they still haven't learned, because the round-belly temptation is simply too strong
variations: Advanced version: showing my belly means I trust you. Petting it means you betrayed that trust. These two facts do not contradict each other.
Short version: the belly is for viewing, not for touching. Learn the difference and you keep your hand.

items.sourceNote: 貓咪半夜三點暴衝（FRAP／zoomies）行為觀察，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
凌晨三點十七分。
整間屋子安靜到聽得見時鐘。

然後——
一陣馬蹄聲從走廊那頭衝過來，
撞倒垃圾桶，繞過沙發，
用時速六十公里衝上你的床，
再從你臉上彈開。

完事後牠若無其事地坐下，開始舔手。
彷彿剛剛什麼都沒發生。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個總是被半夜暴衝吵醒、黑眼圈越來越深的養貓朋友——他會邊笑邊點頭，因為昨晚三點他也剛被主子從臉上踩過去
variations: 進階版：白天睡十八個小時，就是為了把體力存到凌晨三點一次放完。這叫時間管理。
簡短版：牠不是失眠，牠是在履行守夜職責，只是巡邏路線剛好經過你的臉。

en.content:
3:17 in the morning.
The house is so quiet you can hear the clock ticking.

Then —
a thunder of tiny hooves comes charging down the hallway,
knocks over the trash can, banks around the couch,
sprints up onto your bed at 40 miles per hour,
and launches off your face.

Afterward it sits down, calm as anything, and starts grooming a paw.
As if nothing ever happened.
en.editorNote: Send to the cat owner whose eye bags keep getting darker from 3am wake-up calls — they'll laugh and nod, because they got run over across the face at exactly 3am last night too
variations: Advanced version: sleeping eighteen hours a day is just how you bank enough energy to release it all at once at 3am. This is called time management.
Short version: it's not insomnia, it's the night watch. The patrol route just happens to run across your face.

items.sourceNote: 貓咪坐在筆電鍵盤上妨礙工作行為觀察，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
整張桌子這麼大，牠偏偏要坐在鍵盤上。

不是因為鍵盤溫暖，
是因為你的專注冒犯到牠了。

你正在工作，代表你的注意力不在牠身上，
這件事，必須立刻被糾正。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個在家工作、每次開會分享畫面前都要先把貓從鍵盤上抱走的朋友——他懂那種「螢幕一半被毛屁股擋住」的絕望
variations: 進階版：牠會精準地挑你打到一半的那封重要信件坐下去，然後用尾巴幫你多送出三十個字母。這不是意外，這是校稿。
簡短版：你的桌子不是辦公桌，是牠的王座，你只是被允許共用的下屬。

en.content:
The desk is enormous, and yet it chooses to sit right on the keyboard.

Not because the keyboard is warm,
but because your focus offends it.

If you're working, your attention isn't on the cat —
and that, clearly, must be corrected immediately.
en.editorNote: Send to the work-from-home friend who has to peel a cat off the keyboard before every screen-share — they know the exact despair of half the monitor being blocked by a furry butt
variations: Advanced version: it will sit down precisely on the important email you're halfway through, then use its tail to add thirty extra letters for you. This isn't an accident. This is proofreading.
Short version: your desk isn't a workspace, it's a throne. You're just the subordinate allowed to share it.

items.sourceNote: 貓咪把水杯／筆從桌上撥下去以引起注意行為觀察，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
牠學會了一件事：

只要把桌上的東西撥下去，
你就會馬上抬頭、發出聲音、走過來。

所以在牠的世界觀裡，
你的水杯不是水杯，
是一顆「召喚人類」的按鈕。

而牠，非常喜歡按按鈕。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個桌上再也不敢放玻璃杯、改用防摔水壺的朋友——他不是懶得收，是已經認清主子把撥東西當成遙控器在用了
variations: 進階版：牠會先用眼神鎖定你，確認你在看，再慢慢把東西推向桌緣。少了觀眾，這場表演就沒有意義。
簡短版：對牠來說，重力是免費的娛樂，而你的桌面是遊樂場。

en.content:
It has learned one thing:

whenever it swats something off the table,
you instantly look up, make a noise, and come over.

So in its worldview,
your glass of water isn't a glass of water —
it's a 'summon human' button.

And it really, really loves pressing buttons.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who's given up on ever putting a glass on the table again and switched to a spill-proof bottle — they're not lazy, they've simply accepted that the cat treats knocking things off as a remote control
variations: Advanced version: it locks eyes with you first, confirms you're watching, then slowly nudges the object toward the edge. Without an audience, the performance means nothing.
Short version: to the cat, gravity is free entertainment and your tabletop is the playground.

items.sourceNote: 貓咪對主人愛理不理、需要時才靠近行為觀察，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
你叫牠，牠看都不看你一眼。

你不理牠，牠就來坐在你腳邊，
用頭頂你的手，發出呼嚕聲。

這不是善變，
這是主子與生俱來的權力：
什麼時候親近你，由牠決定，不由你。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個叫貓叫到懷疑人生、結果一去廁所貓就跟進來的朋友——他終於明白，主動權從來不在他手上
variations: 進階版：牠不是不愛你，牠是要確保這份愛在牠的時間表上，而不是你的。愛得有排程，才顯得珍貴。
簡短版：你追牠，牠跑；你不理牠，牠來。這就是主子的戀愛守則。

en.content:
You call its name, and it doesn't even glance your way.

You ignore it, and it comes to sit at your feet,
butts its head against your hand, and starts to purr.

This isn't fickleness.
This is a cat's birthright:
it decides when to be close to you — not the other way around.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who's called their cat until they questioned their whole existence, only for it to follow them into the bathroom the second they stopped trying — they finally get that they were never the one in charge
variations: Advanced version: it's not that it doesn't love you, it just needs that love to run on its schedule, not yours. Love with an appointment feels more precious.
Short version: chase the cat, it runs; ignore the cat, it comes. That's the whole feline dating rulebook.

items.sourceNote: 貓咪對昂貴玩具無視、卻愛玩紙箱與塑膠袋行為觀察，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
你花了三百塊買了一個號稱「貓咪最愛」的高級玩具。

牠聞了一下，走開。

然後花了整個下午，
瘋狂鑽進那個玩具寄來時的空紙箱裡，
玩到天黑。

這堂課的成本，三百塊；
學到的道理，無價：
重點從來不是禮物，是包裝。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個貓玩具堆成山、結果貓只玩紙箱和瓶蓋的朋友——他每次剁手都在提醒自己下次別買，但下次還是會買
variations: 進階版：那個紙箱只要一坐得下，就是頂級豪宅；坐不下，牠也要硬塞進去，證明尺寸只是建議。
簡短版：對主子來說，最貴的玩具永遠是免費的那個。

en.content:
You spent ten bucks on a premium toy advertised as 'every cat's favorite.'

It sniffs it once and walks away.

Then it spends the entire afternoon
frantically diving into the empty box the toy shipped in,
playing until it's dark out.

Cost of this lesson: ten bucks.
The wisdom gained: priceless.
It was never about the gift. It was always about the packaging.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend with a mountain of cat toys whose cat only plays with cardboard boxes and bottle caps — every impulse buy is a promise not to do it again, and they'll do it again next week
variations: Advanced version: if the cat can just barely fit in the box, it's a luxury mansion. If it can't fit, it'll cram in anyway to prove that dimensions are merely a suggestion.
Short version: to a cat, the most expensive toy is always the free one.

items.sourceNote: 貓咪的愛與準時開飯掛鉤的觀察，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
牠給你的是無條件的愛。

除非你晚了三分鐘開飯。

那三分鐘裡，
牠會坐在飯碗前直直盯著你，
眼神彷彿在說：「原來我在你心裡，連罐罐都不如。」

所謂無條件，
其實附了一頁密密麻麻的條款，
第一條就是準時。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個手機設了餵貓鬧鐘、還被貓提早半小時叫醒的朋友——他早就認清，這份愛是有服務條款的
variations: 進階版：牠不是不愛你，是愛得很有原則。原則就是：碗要滿、水要新、鏟屎要準時，其餘免談。
簡短版：貓的愛是無條件的，條件是你別遲到。

en.content:
It gives you unconditional love.

Unless you're three minutes late with dinner.

For those three minutes,
it sits in front of the empty bowl and stares you down,
eyes saying: 'So this is where I rank — below a can of wet food.'

Unconditional, sure.
With a dense page of terms attached,
and clause one is: be on time.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who set a feeding alarm and still got woken up half an hour early by their cat — they figured out long ago this love comes with a service agreement
variations: Advanced version: it's not that it doesn't love you, it just loves you with principles. The principles are: full bowl, fresh water, scoop on schedule. Everything else is negotiable.
Short version: a cat's love is unconditional, and the condition is don't be late.

items.sourceNote: 貓咪坐在鍵盤上打斷工作的行為觀察，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
你正在趕一份很重要的報告。

牠跳上桌，
不偏不倚地坐在鍵盤正中央，
屁股對著螢幕，尾巴掃過你的臉。

牠不是在搗亂。

牠是在提醒你：
這世界上有比工作更重要的事，
而那件事，現在正坐在你的鍵盤上。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個開會開到一半、貓突然入鏡搶麥的遠端工作朋友——他早就放棄跟主子搶那張椅子了
variations: 進階版：牠不懂你的報告，但牠很懂一件事——只要牠一坐上鍵盤，你的注意力就百分之百屬於牠。這叫精準打擊。
簡短版：貓不是討厭你工作，牠只是覺得牠比你的工作重要，而牠說得對。

en.content:
You're racing to finish an important report.

It hops onto the desk,
plants itself dead center on the keyboard,
butt to the screen, tail sweeping across your face.

It's not being a nuisance.

It's reminding you:
there are things in this world more important than work,
and right now, one of them is sitting on your keyboard.
en.editorNote: Send to the work-from-home friend whose cat photobombed a meeting and stole the mic — they gave up fighting the boss for that chair ages ago
variations: Advanced version: it doesn't understand your report, but it understands one thing perfectly — the second it sits on the keyboard, one hundred percent of your attention is its. Call it a precision strike.
Short version: the cat doesn't hate your job, it just thinks it's more important than your job, and it's right.

items.sourceNote: 貓咪半夜暴衝、白天狂睡的作息觀察，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
白天，牠睡了十四個小時，
睡到你以為牠是一塊會呼吸的地毯。

凌晨三點，
牠突然像被上了發條，
在走廊來回狂奔，撞倒你的水杯，
然後跳上你的肚子，一臉無辜。

牠不是失眠。

牠只是選在你最需要睡覺的時候，
開始牠精彩的一天。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個被貓半夜三點的貓貓奧運吵醒、還要摸黑找散落一地玩具的朋友——他已經放棄改變主子的作息了
variations: 進階版：你以為那是暴衝，其實那是牠在巡邏牠的領土。而領土的邊界，剛好經過你的臉。
簡短版：貓白天充電，半夜放電，而那個插座就是你的睡眠。

en.content:
During the day, it sleeps fourteen hours,
so still you start to think it's a breathing rug.

At three in the morning,
it suddenly winds up like a toy,
tears up and down the hallway, knocks over your water glass,
then lands on your stomach with a totally innocent face.

It's not insomnia.

It just picks the exact moment you need sleep most
to begin its wonderful day.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend woken up by the 3 a.m. cat olympics who then had to find scattered toys in the dark — they've given up on fixing the boss's schedule
variations: Advanced version: you think it's a case of the zoomies, but really it's patrolling its territory. And the border of that territory runs right across your face.
Short version: the cat charges up all day and discharges at midnight, and the outlet is your sleep.

items.sourceNote: 貓咪對關起來的門的執念，門開了又不進去的行為觀察

zh-tw.content:
只要你關上任何一扇門，
主子就會出現在門的另一邊，
用生命在抓門。

但當你終於開了門，
牠會站在門口，
看看裡面，看看外面，
然後決定——
哪邊都不去。

牠要的不是進來，
是門必須為牠而開。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個家裡每扇門都不能關的朋友——連上廁所都要開一條縫給主子監督的那種
variations: 延伸版：門是關的，牠要進去。門是開的，牠要出來。真正讓牠不爽的不是門的方向，是這扇門沒有先問過牠。

en.content:
Close any door in the house,
and the cat instantly materializes on the other side,
clawing at it like its life depends on it.

But the moment you open it,
the cat stands in the doorway,
looks inside, looks outside,
and decides:
neither.

It never wanted to come in.
It wanted the door to open for it.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who can't close a single door at home — the kind who leaves the bathroom cracked open for feline supervision
variations: Extended version: door closed, it wants in. Door open, it wants out. What actually offends it isn't the direction of the door — it's that the door was operated without its approval.

items.sourceNote: 「我付帳單，貓管家」主題，主子掌權、飼主打工的關係

zh-tw.content:
這個家是主子的。

我只是負責付房貸、繳水電、
買飼料、鏟屎、
以及在半夜三點準時提供陪玩服務。

我不是主人，
我是這個家唯一的員工。

而且沒有年終。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個嘴上說「養貓」、其實是被貓養著的朋友，讓他認清自己的職稱
variations: 延伸版：績效考核由主子親自進行，標準是——你今天的服務有沒有讓牠不爽。通常都有。

en.content:
This house belongs to the cat.

I just handle the mortgage, the utilities,
the food, the litter box,
and the on-call playtime service at 3 a.m. sharp.

I'm not the owner.
I'm the sole employee.

And there's no year-end bonus.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who claims they 'have a cat' — gently remind them of their actual job title
variations: Extended version: performance reviews are conducted by the cat personally. The only metric is whether today's service annoyed it. It usually did.

items.sourceNote: 「無條件的愛，除非晚餐遲到」——貓的愛附帶條款

zh-tw.content:
主子對你的愛，是無條件的。

條件是：
飯要準時。
水要新鮮。
零食要隨傳隨到。
你摸牠的時候牠剛好想被摸。

以上任一條沒達成，
愛就會暫時凍結，
直到你補上為止。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個飯晚了五分鐘就被主子在腳邊碎念的朋友——他懂什麼叫附帶條款的愛
variations: 延伸版：牠不是不愛你，牠只是把愛設成了訂閱制，而你剛好忘記續費。

en.content:
The cat's love for you is unconditional.

Terms and conditions:
Meals must be on time.
Water must be fresh.
Treats must be available on demand.
And it must want to be petted at the exact moment you pet it.

Fail any one of these,
and the love is temporarily frozen
until you make it right.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend whose cat starts complaining at their feet when dinner is five minutes late — they understand love with a fine print
variations: Extended version: it's not that the cat doesn't love you. It's that the love is a subscription, and you forgot to renew it.

items.sourceNote: 貓自以為很會躲、其實半個身體露在外面的行為觀察

zh-tw.content:
主子躲起來的時候，
堅信自己完全隱形。

事實是：
頭埋在紙箱裡，
屁股和整條尾巴露在外面，
還會不小心動一下。

但你不能戳破。
在牠的世界裡，牠是忍者。
你必須配合演出，假裝找不到。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個貓躲在窗簾後面、只露出兩隻腳還以為沒人看見的朋友
variations: 延伸版：規則是這樣的——只要主子看不到你，牠就認定你也看不到牠。這是牠的物理學，不接受質疑。

en.content:
When the cat hides,
it is fully convinced it has turned invisible.

The reality:
head buried in a cardboard box,
butt and entire tail sticking out,
twitching every few seconds.

But you can't call it out.
In its world, it's a ninja.
You have to play along and pretend you can't find it.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend whose cat hides behind the curtain with both feet showing, certain no one can see it
variations: Extended version: the rule is simple — if the cat can't see you, it assumes you can't see it either. That's its physics, and it's not up for debate.

items.sourceNote: 「我假裝不喜歡你，卻坐在你的鍵盤上」——貓表達愛的矛盾方式

zh-tw.content:
主子的愛，表達方式如下：

你叫牠，牠不理。
你不理牠，牠來坐在你正在打字的鍵盤上。

你想抱牠，牠掙脫。
你在忙，牠整隻趴在你臉上。

牠不是不愛你。
牠只是堅持要在你最不方便的時候愛你。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個一開電腦，貓就準時坐上鍵盤的朋友——這不是干擾，這是愛的表現
variations: 延伸版：主子的深情，永遠出現在你最沒空的那一刻。這不是巧合，這是牠精算過的時機。

en.content:
Here's how the cat expresses love:

You call it, it ignores you.
You ignore it, it comes and sits on the keyboard you're typing on.

You try to hold it, it squirms away.
You're busy, it drapes itself across your entire face.

It's not that it doesn't love you.
It just insists on loving you at the most inconvenient possible moment.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend whose cat sits on the keyboard the second the laptop opens — that's not interference, that's affection
variations: Extended version: the cat's deepest devotion always arrives at the exact moment you have no time for it. That's not a coincidence. That's carefully calculated timing.

items.sourceNote: 花大錢買貓窩，主子卻只睡紙箱——養貓人共同的經濟學悲劇

zh-tw.content:
你花了兩千塊買了一個高級貓窩。
羊毛內裡、記憶棉、還有主子專屬的挑高涼亭設計。

主子睡在紙箱裡。
順便睡在你買貓窩時附贈的那張塑膠膜上。

那個貓窩，最後變成你放雜物的地方。
主子偶爾會坐上去——用來監視你有沒有把牠的紙箱丟掉。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個剛失心瘋買了昂貴貓窩、結果貓只愛紙箱的朋友——這筆學費，每個貓奴都繳過
variations: 延伸版：貓窩的正確用途，是拿來裝那個你以為主子會喜歡、但牠一眼都不看的玩具。它們會在角落一起積灰。

en.content:
You spent sixty dollars on a premium cat bed.
Wool lining, memory foam, and a raised gazebo design just for your overlord.

The cat sleeps in the cardboard box.
And on the plastic wrap that came with the bed.

That cat bed eventually becomes where you store random junk.
The cat occasionally sits on it — to monitor whether you've thrown out its box.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who just impulse-bought an expensive cat bed only for the cat to claim the box it shipped in — a tuition every cat owner has paid
variations: Extended version: the true purpose of a cat bed is to hold the toy you were sure your cat would love but never looked at once. They gather dust together in the corner.

items.sourceNote: 凌晨三點暴衝（zoomies）——主子的固定夜間節目

zh-tw.content:
白天的主子：躺著。躺著。換個地方躺著。
電量顯示：3%。

凌晨三點的主子：
以時速六十公里橫越客廳，
用頭撞開房門，
從你的臉上起跑衝向衣櫃頂端。
電量顯示：滿格，且來源不明。

沒有人知道那股能量是哪來的。
連牠自己也不知道。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個被貓半夜暴衝踩醒、從此再也睡不好的朋友——這不是意外，這是每晚準時上演的節目
variations: 延伸版：科學無法解釋主子凌晨三點的能量從哪來。唯一確定的是，白天充的電都是為了這一刻。

en.content:
Daytime cat: lying down. Lying down. Relocating to lie down elsewhere.
Battery reading: 3%.

Cat at 3 a.m.:
crossing the living room at forty miles an hour,
headbutting the bedroom door open,
using your face as a launchpad toward the top of the wardrobe.
Battery reading: full, source unknown.

Nobody knows where that energy comes from.
Not even the cat.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who's been trampled awake by 3 a.m. zoomies and never slept soundly since — this isn't an accident, it's a nightly scheduled program
variations: Extended version: science cannot explain where a cat sources its 3 a.m. energy. The only certainty is that all the daytime charging was for this single moment.

items.sourceNote: 「養貓後你會靠掉落聲辨認物品」——養貓人的特殊技能

zh-tw.content:
養貓久了，你會練成一種特殊技能：

光靠東西掉到地上的聲音，
就能判斷主子這次推下去的是什麼。

「叩」是遙控器。
「鏘」是玻璃杯。
「啪嗒」是你放在桌邊的手機。
那個很長的沉默之後，才傳來的巨響——
是你最貴的那個。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個聽到聲音不用回頭、就知道貓又推了什麼下去的朋友——這是養貓人才懂的絕活
variations: 延伸版：真正的高手，聽聲音就能同時判斷是什麼掉了、掉在哪、以及還救不救得回來。主子則負責在旁邊看你收拾，眼神毫無悔意。

en.content:
Live with a cat long enough and you develop a special skill:

you can identify exactly what the cat just knocked down
purely by the sound it makes hitting the floor.

'Thunk' is the remote.
'Clink' is a glass.
'Clack' is the phone you left near the edge.
And that long silence followed by a huge crash —
that's the most expensive thing you own.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who no longer even turns around, because they already know from the sound what the cat pushed off this time — a talent only cat owners understand
variations: Extended version: a true expert can tell from the sound alone what fell, where it landed, and whether it's still salvageable. The cat, meanwhile, watches you clean up with zero remorse in its eyes.

items.sourceNote: 貓總在你視訊會議時開始鬼叫——主子的精準干擾能力

zh-tw.content:
主子有一種內建雷達，
專門偵測你「絕對不能被打擾」的時刻。

你整個上午在家，牠睡死。
你視訊會議一開始分享畫面，
牠準時站到門口，
開始用最淒厲的音量，
對著一面空白的牆壁高歌。

全公司的人都聽到了。
主子毫無愧色，因為牠覺得那是你的問題。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個一開會、貓就準時開唱的遠端工作朋友——這不是巧合，這是主子精算過的時機
variations: 延伸版：主子的干擾從來不是隨機的。牠會挑最安靜、最正式、最無法忽視的那一刻登場。這是牠對「你居然把注意力放在別人身上」的抗議。

en.content:
Cats come with a built-in radar
specifically for detecting the moment you absolutely cannot be interrupted.

You're home all morning, it sleeps like the dead.
The second you share your screen on a video call,
it walks to the doorway, right on cue,
and begins belting out its most bloodcurdling aria
at a completely blank wall.

The whole company heard it.
The cat feels no shame, because it considers that your problem.
en.editorNote: Send to the remote-work friend whose cat starts its concert the instant a meeting begins — not a coincidence, but carefully calculated timing
variations: Extended version: a cat's interruptions are never random. It picks the quietest, most formal, most impossible-to-ignore moment to make its entrance. It's a protest against you daring to put your attention on someone else.

items.sourceNote: 貓盯著空無一物的角落——養貓人的日常驚悚

zh-tw.content:
半夜，主子突然坐直，
盯著房間某個角落。
那裡什麼都沒有。

耳朵轉來轉去，尾巴慢慢擺動，
瞳孔放到最大，
目光鎖定在一片空白的牆面上。

你順著牠的視線看過去。
什麼都沒有。
真的什麼都沒有。
……你決定今晚開燈睡。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個曾經被貓盯空氣嚇到的朋友——養貓的人，都被主子的「見鬼視線」寒毛豎起過至少一次
variations: 延伸版：主子盯著空角落時，你有兩個選擇：假裝沒看到，或者跟牠一起盯。無論選哪個，你今晚都不會睡得太安穩。

en.content:
In the middle of the night, the cat suddenly sits bolt upright,
staring at a corner of the room.
There is nothing there.

Its ears swivel, its tail sways slowly,
its pupils blow wide open,
its gaze locked onto a blank stretch of wall.

You follow its line of sight.
Nothing.
Absolutely nothing.
… You decide to sleep with the light on tonight.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who's been spooked by their cat staring at thin air — every cat owner has had their hair stand on end from that 'seeing ghosts' stare at least once
variations: Extended version: when a cat stares at an empty corner, you have two options — pretend you didn't notice, or stare with it. Either way, you're not sleeping well tonight.
